description.pageDescription:
This is a striking monochromatic e-commerce site with a sophisticated dark theme. The layout follows a modular grid system showcasing digital products and mockups.

Layout breakdown:
- Header: Minimal top bar with Menu and Cart
- Hero section: Features a large T-shirt mockup with "027" branding
- Main content: Organized in a clean 3-column grid of product cards
- Each product card displays:
  - Product image in grayscale
  - Product name (numbered series from 001-027)
  - Price point (consistently $4-$9 range)
  - Hover states likely reveal additional details

Content types include:
- Product mockups (t-shirts, digital tablets, bottles, packaging)
- Digital assets (components, text effects, mockup templates)
- Physical product renders
- Cookie consent notice
- Newsletter signup footer
- Payment method icons

Visual elements:
- Stark black background (#000000)
- High contrast white text and elements
- Consistent numerical branding (001-027 system)
- Minimal product photography
- Strong use of negative space
- Monospaced typography throughout
- Clean grid layout with consistent spacing
description.style: Modern brutalist with a strong minimalist approach. The design embraces digital-first aesthetics with a focus on typography and negative space. The numbered product system creates a cohesive, archive-like feel.
description.audience: Digital creators, designers, and creative professionals looking for high-quality mockups and digital assets. Appeals to those who appreciate minimal, sophisticated design.
description.layoutStyle: Contemporary experimental grid layout that breaks from traditional e-commerce conventions while maintaining clear hierarchy and usability.
description.intent:
- Primary: Sell digital design assets and mockups
- Secondary: Position the brand as a curator of premium design resources
- Create an exclusive, gallery-like shopping experience
- Drive newsletter signups for marketing

The site uses systematic numbering and consistent pricing to create a cohesive product collection feel, more reminiscent of an art gallery than a typical e-commerce store.

description.pageDescription:
The page features a meticulously organized color palette showcase laid out in a clean 3x10 grid system. At the top, there's a simple header with the title "Mindfully curated color palettes" in an elegant serif typeface. Below that is explanatory text suggesting these are "color combinations for your design projects."

The main content consists of 30 numbered color swatches arranged in a consistent grid with ample whitespace between each item. Each swatch is a perfect square with rounded corners, displaying a number in varying typography styles. The color selections range from soft pastels to bold primaries, creating a sophisticated and versatile collection. Some swatches feature subtle gradients or circular elements that add depth and visual interest.

The spacing is precise and generous, with consistent margins between all elements. The grid layout maintains perfect alignment both horizontally and vertically. There's a clear visual hierarchy with the title at top, followed by the subtitle, and then the organized grid of swatches below.
description.style: Minimalist and modern with a touch of sophisticated elegance. The design employs principles of Swiss/International Style with its grid-based layout and typography-forward approach.
description.audience: Creative professionals, designers, and artists who need carefully curated color combinations for their projects. The sophisticated presentation suggests it's aimed at design-conscious individuals who appreciate refined color theory.
description.pageType: Resource/tool page - specifically a color palette library or collection
description.layoutStyle: Contemporary and structured, following modernist principles with a clear grid system. The approach is clean and systematic while maintaining visual interest through color variation.
description.intent:
To provide designers and creatives with thoughtfully selected color combinations they can use in their work. The page aims to be both a practical tool and a source of color inspiration, encouraging users to "steal them" and "make something beautiful" as stated in the subtitle.

The combination of fonts (Times, Nunito, MaisonNeue-Light, Louize, Avenir) creates a sophisticated typographic hierarchy that balances classical and contemporary styles, further emphasizing the curated nature of the collection.

description.pageDescription:
The page appears to be a gallery-style layout showcasing a collection of design work and device mockups. The structure follows a masonry-style grid layout with varying image sizes organized in a responsive manner. The page consists of:

- Header with simple navigation
- Main content area displaying a comprehensive grid of project images
- Multiple rows of photographs showing devices, UI designs, and architectural/environmental shots
- Strong emphasis on documentation-style photography of digital devices and screens
- High contrast between dark and light elements
- Consistent spacing between grid items
- Mix of landscape and portrait orientations
- Heavy focus on showing work in context through environmental photography

The content appears to be organized chronologically or by project, with careful attention to visual rhythm and pacing. Images show various angles and perspectives of what seems to be an art direction or design project, including:
- Device screens and interfaces
- Environmental/architectural photography
- Detail shots of typography and signage
- Interactive elements and UI components
- Documentary-style process shots
description.style: Contemporary minimalist with strong documentary/archival influences. The aesthetic is clean and systematic while maintaining a raw, behind-the-scenes quality. There's a clear focus on process and presentation rather than polish.
description.audience:
Design professionals, particularly:
- Art directors
- UI/UX designers
- Creative directors
- Design students
- Digital designers looking for documentation inspiration
description.pageType: Portfolio/Case study gallery page showcasing a specific project or body of work
description.layoutStyle: Modern gallery-grid layout with experimental documentation approach. The layout prioritizes visual storytelling through careful image sequencing and sizing relationships.
description.intent:
To document and present a comprehensive view of a design project's development and implementation, with particular emphasis on:
- Showing work in context
- Documenting the design process
- Demonstrating attention to detail
- Presenting multiple viewpoints and applications
- Creating a visual narrative of the project's scope

The page aims to give viewers deep insight into the design process while maintaining a clean, systematic presentation that emphasizes the work itself.

metadata.title: ART+TECH Report | Digital Art Coll3cting
metadata.description: In this latest edition, the ART+TECH Report | Digital Art Coll3cting explores the rapidly evolving world of web3 and its impact on the art market and the concept of art collecting, delving deep into the buying patterns and collecting motivations of art enthusiasts and Digital Art collectors.
metadata.ogDescription: In this latest edition, the ART+TECH Report | Digital Art Coll3cting explores the rapidly evolving world of web3 and its impact on the art market and the concept of art collecting, delving deep into the buying patterns and collecting motivations of art enthusiasts and Digital Art collectors.
metadata.textContent:
DIGITAL
ART
COLL3CTING
DOWNLOAD FULL REPORT
DIGITAL
ART
COLL3CTING
DOWNLOAD FULL REPORT
ART+TECH REPORT
DIGITAL
ART
COLL3CTING
2023 EDITION
INTRODUCTION

Web3 and blockchain technology have the potential to change the art industry in many ways, revolutionizing the way art is created, owned, and experienced as well as providing new opportunities for artists and collectors. This will undoubtedly lead to new forms of collecting and a new diversity of collectors entering the art market. 

In this latest edition, the ART+TECH Report | Digital Art Coll3cting explores the rapidly evolving world of web3 and its impact on the art market and the concept of art collecting, delving deep into the buying patterns and collecting motivations of art enthusiasts and Digital Art collectors.

From April to July 2023, we conducted an online survey involving 300 global Digital Art collectors who generously shared their thoughts with us. The report was published in October 2023.

SOCIO —
DEMOGRAPHIC
GENDER (%)
AGE IN YEARS  (%)
AVERAGE PRICE SPENT PER DIGITAL ARTWORK
DIGITAL ART COLLECTING EXPERIENCES IN YEARS
COLLECTING ACTIVIES
METHODOLOGY

As framework of the ART+TECH Report | Digital Art Coll3cting, 300 international art and art NFT collectors were surveyed about their personal buying experiences and collecting motivations with Digital Art.

The online survey was conducted between June 8th and July 31st, 2023 (using the Typeform software). By distributing the survey link in cooperation with renowned art and NFT experts, influencing communities and via Twitter, Discord and LinkedIn, we were able to address a correspondingly broad collector base.

In the questionnaire, respondents could identify themselves as male,  female, other or could prefer not to say. Those who identified as male or female were split in 55% and 39% respectively, with 0% selected other and 6% preferred not to say and included a variety of age groups and collector profiles. Among the 300 respondents surveyed, 80 % identified as experienced Digital Art collectors and form the basis for the following questions on art NFT collecting.

In this report, we focus on Digital Art as digital artworks that are created, presented, and traded using NFT and blockchain technology. Naturally, we want to avoid a restrictive definition of what counts as “art” and what does not. But for practical purposes, we’re using the term “art NFT” and “Digital Art” to single out types of NFTs and collectibles that are collected in large editions and by a specific audience with distinct motivations.

All quotes are taken from anonymous, unaltered comments that were provided by respondents who took the online survey.

This is an independent report and was not commissioned by any specific institution or sponsor. It therefore serves as a general reference and actual results are not guaranteed.

KEY FINDINGS

THE MOST IMPORTANT FINDINGS ARE SUMMARISED
IN THE FOLLOWING 9 CHAPTERS.

01
DIGITAL ART
JOINS THE ART CANON
02
THE RISE OF GENART
03
CRYPTO CRASH
≠ ART NFT CRASH
04
WEB3 RULES / ROLES APPLY

05
POWER TO THE ARTIST
06
CURATION & CONTEXTUALISATION
REMAIN KEY
07
THE WEIGHT OF COMMUNITIES
08
COLLECTIVE OWNERSHIP:
A NEW FORM OF ART COLLECTING
09
LIFE AFTER THE HYPE
01
DIGITAL ART
JOINS THE ART CANON
83% AGREE THAT DIGITAL ART
HOLDS THE SAME SIGNIFICANCE
AS TRADITIONAL ART FORMS

In this survey, 83% of respondents agree that Digital Art holds the same significance as traditional art forms like painting or sculpture. For 86% of all collectors the appreciation of Digital Art is the number one collecting motivation, and 55% of respondents stated that their personal perception of the significance of Digital Art has increased in the last year. For many years, Digital Art has struggled with value appreciation and ownership challenges, but digital ownership associated with blockchain technology has finally anchored it in the official art canon and given it its significance and merits it certainly deserves.

[ 01 ]

“Finally it is officially perceived (in value and meaning)
in line with traditional art.”

02
THE RISE
OF GENART
OVER HALF OF THE COLLECTORS FOCUS
ON BLOCKCHAIN-BASED ART

In this study, Generative Art received the highest collecting focus (55%), indicating its significance as a leading Digital Art form, with Blockchain-based art coming in second (48%). The advancement of Generative Art can certainly be attributed to artists' increasing recognition of blockchain as a dynamic creative medium, resulting in entirely new artistic expressions; undeniably also fascinating for collectors with a genuine appreciation for Digital Art. Interestingly, AI Art, despite its novelty, ranks fourth and certainly stands to gain from the current AI momentum. In this survey, fxhash and ArtBlocks are the top platforms for collecting Generative Art.

[ 02 ]

“Generative Art has a unique opportunity to comment on the everchanging relationship between human and machine.”

03
CRYPTO CRASH
≠ ART NFT CRASH
MORE THAN

description.pageDescription:
The page follows a bold, modern layout featuring a striking electric blue background color scheme. The design employs a multi-column grid system with generous whitespace and clear hierarchical organization. 

Key sections include:
- Header with "DIGITAL ART COLLECTING" in white typography
- Large text blocks with excellent contrast against the blue background
- Data visualization section featuring minimalist bar charts and statistics
- "KEY FINDINGS" section with numbered items (01-09)
- Infographic elements showing percentages and comparative data
- Clean, structured content blocks with clear typographic hierarchy

The layout maintains strong visual rhythm through consistent spacing and alignment. Content is presented in digestible chunks with clear separation between sections. The design makes effective use of white text against the vibrant blue background, creating high readability despite the bold color choice.

Interactive elements appear to include subtle hover states on buttons and links, though this static image doesn't show the full range of interactions.
description.style: Modern minimalist with a tech-forward aesthetic. The design embraces contemporary digital art themes through its bold use of color and clean typography. The style feels cutting-edge and authoritative while maintaining professional polish.
description.audience: Digital art collectors, crypto/NFT enthusiasts, art technology professionals, and contemporary art market participants. The design suggests a technically-savvy audience interested in the intersection of art and technology.
description.pageType: Research report landing page / digital whitepaper presentation
description.layoutStyle: Modern and experimental, pushing beyond conventional layout patterns while maintaining strong usability. The single-color background with white text is a bold choice that sets this apart from standard report designs.
description.intent:
The page aims to present key findings from what appears to be an art technology report, likely focused on digital art collecting trends. The goal seems to be establishing authority in the digital art space while making complex data accessible and engaging. The design encourages users to read through the findings and potentially download or access the full report.

The typographic choices (Montserrat and Instrumentsans) reinforce the modern, technical feel while ensuring readability. The variable font usage suggests attention to responsive design and modern web typography practices.

Header:
- Simple text "Bruno Arizio" on the left
- "Selected Works" in center
- "General Info" and time (CET 07:03 AM) on right
- Clean, sans-serif typography using HelveticaNowText

Main Content:
- Left sidebar features small thumbnail project previews arranged vertically
- Large central/right area displays a dominant black and white artistic photograph showing abstract texture/movement
- Project preview visible showing "Van Moof" product landing page with skincare-related content
- Text overlay reading "Treat your skin in every season" with "Garoa" branding
- Contact information "For inquiries: hello@brunoarizio.com" placed discreetly

Visual Elements:
- Monochromatic color palette (black, white, grays)
- Heavy use of negative space
- High contrast photography
- Minimal interface elements
- No obvious decorative elements or borders
- Clean typography with careful attention to hierarchy

Interactions appear subtle and understated, likely with minimal hover states and smooth transitions between project views.

description.pageDescription:
This is a beautifully minimal e-commerce website for an artist selling prints and wallpapers. The layout follows a clean, gallery-style grid system with consistent spacing and strong visual hierarchy.

Sections:
- Header: Minimal navigation with Store/About links and cart icon
- New Arrivals: Horizontal scrollable gallery of artwork
- Main Content Area: Split between Prints and Wallpapers sections
- Featured Works: Large showcase of selected pieces
- Best Sellers: Another horizontal scrollable gallery
- Footer: Simple newsletter signup and minimal links

The design emphasizes the artwork through generous whitespace and a pristine white background. The products themselves feature striking gradient compositions with fluid, organic shapes in vibrant colors - primarily blues, reds, pinks, and purples. Each piece is displayed as a clean thumbnail with minimal text (title and price).

The layout uses subtle hover states on product cards and implements smooth scrolling navigation. Pricing is consistently displayed as "from €60.00" format under each piece.
description.style: Ultra-minimalist, contemporary gallery aesthetic with strong emphasis on the artwork. The design steps back to let the colorful, abstract artworks be the focal point. Clean, sophisticated, and deliberately understated.
description.audience: Design-conscious consumers, interior designers, and art collectors who appreciate contemporary abstract art and high-end digital prints. Appeals to those with sophisticated taste in modern design and home décor.
description.layoutStyle: Modern and gallery-inspired, using contemporary e-commerce patterns but with an artistic twist. The horizontal scrolling galleries and generous whitespace create an elevated shopping experience.
description.intent:
The primary goal is to showcase and sell the artist's prints and wallpapers while maintaining a high-end, gallery-like atmosphere. The design aims to position the works as premium art pieces rather than mass-market decorations. The clean layout encourages browsing and discovery while making the purchase process clear and straightforward.

The design successfully balances commercial functionality with artistic presentation, creating an experience that feels more like visiting a digital gallery than a typical online store.

metadata.title: Carrot ‣ Carrot
metadata.description: Our software collects waste data on a tenant-level, giving companies and households a motivation to reduce their own waste
metadata.ogDescription: Our software collects waste data on a tenant-level, giving companies and households a motivation to reduce their own waste
h1s: Closing the circularity gap by rewarding sustainable behaviour.
"Carrot is a game changer in how we work with and report waste."
h2s: Collecting data about waste at Vestkanten Storsenter enabled more sustainable choices and a significant increase in the recycling rates.
Data can change how you think about waste.
metadata.textContent:
Product 
Our why
About us
Contact
Book a meeting
Closing the circularity gap by rewarding sustainable behaviour. 

Our software collects waste data on a tenant-level, giving companies and people a motivation to reduce their own waste – a bit like a Carrot.
Get in touch!

Flexible data collection

Thanks to integrations with market leading hardware vendors, combined with our own registration suite, we gather all data in one platform at the point of disposal. With no hardware dependencies, Carrot works with any waste setup and waste collection process.

Data-driven improvements

Better data allows for more targeted follow-up. We help you become more data-driven in managing and optimising your building from a waste perspective, providing you with immediate insights for waste setup, tenant follow-up, cost reduction, reporting, and beyond.

Empowered tenants

What gets measured, gets improved. With Carrot, all of your tenants get immediate access to their own waste data in real time, helping them understand their performance and how they compare, thereby fostering a move towards more sustainable behaviour.

Commercial Real Estate

With waste data on a tenant level, Carrot can make waste handling more sustainable, less costly, easier to report on, and possible to do something about.

Discover how

Residential real estate

With out-of-the-box integrations to digital infrastructure, Carrot can help municipal waste managers unleash the power of data to motivate people to reduce waste and improve sorting.

Discover how

How it works

Carrot Insight

Analytics interface with overview of waste amounts and recycling performance for all your buildings and tenants, with improvement suggestions and smart insights.

Carrot for Tenants

Giving each tenant immediate access to their own waste data to create engagement on a tenant-level, with waste readily available for sustainability reporting and waste reduction measures.

Registration suite

Seamless manual or semi-manual waste registration for tenants and service providers, working with any type of weighing equipment and any waste collection process.

The Carrot API

Integrating with data from any smart waste hardware to enable automatic data capture – and integrating all Carrot data into your own systems for reporting, cost distribution, and insight.

Collecting data about waste at Vestkanten Storsenter enabled more sustainable choices and a significant increase in the recycling rates.

Before Carrot

54% sorting rate

After Carrot

72% sorting rate

Read the case study
Selected clients and partners
Benefits

Actionable data at your fingertips

Understand immediate quick wins-for improving recycling rate and reducing waste, including smart suggestions on waste streams based on your specific building.

Over time, delve into more comprehensive analysis, such as tenant activity, comparative building performance, leaderboards, and underperforming areas.

Cost reduction and financial gains

Reduce volumes of general waste to cut down on haulage costs – a considerable factor as CO2 taxes on waste escalate. Secure green loans for your building using Carrot data for documentation.

Tenant-specific waste data also enables new financial models for incentivising tenants to improve and reduce the waste they generate.

Future-proof waste reporting

Effortlessly generate detailed reports with the necessary data at your disposal.

Select tenants, time frame, waste types, and aggregation level to suit your requirements, and make the data available through multi-format exports or the Carrot API.

Efficient, behavior-changing follow-up

Data at a tenant-level means you can understand instantly what tenants that actually need follow-up, allowing for more targeted follow-up.

Increasing accountability brings motivation for the tenants to change their behaviour, helping you achieve improved recycling rates and reduced general waste.

"Carrot is a game changer in how we work with and report waste."

- Energy & Sustainability Lead

Large UK Facility Manager

Data can change how you think about waste. 

Get in touch to understand how our data can help you reduce waste, meet reporting requirements and save costs.

The page follows a modern, scrolling single-page layout with a strong yellow/chartreuse background color scheme. The layout uses a clear grid system with generous whitespace and distinct sections:

- Header section features bold typography with a mission statement and silhouette graphic
- Product showcase section with three horizontally aligned device mockups (desktop, laptop, mobile)
- Two distinct real estate sections (Commercial and Residential) with high-quality photography
- "How it Works" section with a step-by-step timeline visualization
- Benefits section using a card-based layout with icons and text
- Footer with contact information and partner logos

The visual hierarchy is well-defined through typography scale, with large headlines contrasting against smaller body text. Content blocks are organized in a clean, asymmetrical grid that maintains breathing room between elements.

Interactive elements include:
- Black button CTAs with hover states
- Navigation menu items

- Smooth scroll transitions between sections

Visual elements combine minimalist black and white elements against the distinctive yellow background, creating high contrast and visual interest. Photography is used strategically to show real-world applications.

metadata.title: Cassette - A Music Curation Agency
metadata.description: Cassette - A Music Curation Agency creates sonic identities for hotels, restaurants, retail, real estate properties and more. Our handcrafted mixes don’t just fill a room—they set the tone, enhance atmosphere, and create lasting connections between brands and their guests.
metadata.ogDescription: Cassette - A Music Curation Agency creates sonic identities for hotels, restaurants, retail, real estate properties and more. Our handcrafted mixes don’t just fill a room—they set the tone, enhance atmosphere, and create lasting connections between brands and their guests.
h2s: Cassette shapes how brands are heard and felt.
Trusted by brands that know every track matters.
Music lovers, brand builders, and space shapers. The right people for the right sound.
We don’t “generate.” We give your space its soundtrack.
The experience starts with what people hear.
metadata.textContent:
LOS ANGELES
NEW YORK
LONDON
PARIS
ABOUT
CLIENTS
TEAM
SERVICES
CONTACT
WHY
Sound that speaks for you.
ABOUT CASSETTE
A MUSIC CURATION AGENCY
Cassette shapes how brands are heard and felt.
ALGORITHMS PLAY IT SAFE. WE PLAY IT RIGHT.

AS A MUSIC CURATION AGENCY, OUR HANDCRAFTED MIXES GIVE SPACES THEIR BEAT. THEY SET THE TONE, UNDERSCORE MOMENTS, AND MAKE BRANDS UNFORGETTABLE.

EVERYTHING WE DO IS DESIGNED WITH INTENTION, SHAPED BY HUMANS, TO EVOKE EMOTION AND ENHANCE EXPERIENCE. OUR GLOBAL TEAM BRINGS DECADES OF GOOD BAGGAGE TO THE TASK, HAVING WORKED ACROSS MUSIC, HOSPITALITY, DESIGN, AND BRAND STRATEGY.

AND THAT STANDS NO MATTER WHAT KIND OF SPACE. FOR HOTELS, RESTAURANTS, RETAIL STORES, WELLNESS CENTERS, OFFICE BUILDINGS, MEMBERSHIP CLUBS AND MORE. WE APPROACH SOUND AS A CRUCIAL PIECE OF EVERY SPACE’S STORY.

BECAUSE IT’S NOT JUST ABOUT HOW THE MUSIC SOUNDS—IT’S ABOUT WHAT IT MAKES POSSIBLE.
WHY WE DO IT
OUR
CLIENTS
Trusted by brands that know every track matters.
OUR
TEAM
Music lovers, brand builders, and space shapers. The right people for the right sound.
TEAM
Andrew Wintner
Founder & CEO
ANDREW@CASSETTEMUSIC.COM

ANDREW HAS SPENT HIS CAREER SHAPING HOW MUSIC MOVES THROUGH SPACES. AS THE FOUNDER AND CEO OF CASSETTE, HE FOUND THE PERFECT INTERSECTION OF HIS TWO OBSESSIONS—MUSIC AND HOSPITALITY.

BEFORE LAUNCHING CASSETTE, HE BUILT A CAREER IN BRAND MARKETING AND SALES FOR HOSPITALITY ICONS LIKE THE PALMS HOTEL & CASINO, GERBER GROUP, B.R. GUEST RESTAURANTS, AND MARRIOTT HOTELS. A FORMER DJ AND LIFELONG MIXTAPE OBSESSIVE, HE TURNED THAT PASSION INTO HIS FIRST VENTURE IN THE BACKGROUND MUSIC SPACE WITH THE BEAT ADVISORY IN 2010, LATER SELLING IT TO UK-BASED MUSICSTYLING, WHERE HE RAN U.S. OPERATIONS FOR FIVE YEARS. IN 2019, HE LAUNCHED TALEA (NOW CASSETTE) TO CREATE THE KIND OF AGENCY HE ALWAYS WANTED TO HIRE—ONE THAT TREATS MUSIC AS PART OF THE DESIGN, IDENTITY, AND EMOTION OF A SPACE, NOT JUST BACKGROUND NOISE.

HE'S BEEN IN THE GAME LONG ENOUGH TO KNOW WHAT WORKS—AND WHAT DOESN’T. THAT’S WHAT MAKES CASSETTE DIFFERENT.

Elias Constantopedos
Chief Product Officer & Partner
ELIAS@CASSETTEMUSIC.COM

ELIAS DESIGNS THE TOOLS THAT BRING YOUR SPACE’S SOUND TO LIFE. A TRAINED CONCERT PIANIST AND COMPOSER, HE UNDERSTANDS MUSIC FROM THE INSIDE OUT—BOTH HOW IT’S MADE AND HOW IT’S EXPERIENCED. FOR THE PAST 15 YEARS, HE’S WORKED ACROSS MUSIC, ENTERTAINMENT, AND ADVERTISING, BUILDING APPS AND DIGITAL PRODUCTS FOR THE LIKES OF MARVEL, SONY, AND DISNEY. HE’S AS COMFORTABLE SKETCHING UX AS HE IS TALKING COMPRESSION RATIOS, AND WITH A MASTER’S IN MUSIC TECHNOLOGY FROM NYU, HE’S THE RARE KIND OF PRODUCT DESIGNER WHO CAN HEAR WHAT MOST PEOPLE MISS.

Jamie Hampton
Director of Operations
JAMIE@CASSETTEMUSIC.COM

JAMIE’S BEEN MAKING PLAYLISTS FOR AS LONG AS SHE CAN REMEMBER. WHAT STARTED WITH BURNED CDS FOR FRIENDS TURNED INTO A CAREER TURNING BRAND SPACES INTO SOMETHING PEOPLE FEEL. WITH A JOURNALISM DEGREE FROM THE UNIVERSITY OF WYOMING AND YEARS SPENT LISTENING HARDER THAN MOST, SHE’S A CHAMPION FOR MUSIC THAT SAYS SOMETHING—EVEN IF IT’S PLAYING QUIETLY IN THE CORNER.

Isaac Bachicha
Director of Music Programming
ISAAC@CASSETTEMUSIC.COM

ISAAC’S BEEN DJING ROOMS AND RUNNING MUSIC STRATEGY LONG ENOUGH TO KNOW THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN GOOD AND UNFORGETTABLE. WITH A CAREER SPANNING TOP NIGHTLIFE BRANDS AND GLOBAL COMPANIES, HE BRINGS A DEEP KNOWLEDGE OF WHAT MAKES PEOPLE STAY—AND THE TECHNICAL KNOW-HOW TO MAKE IT ALL WORK BEHIND THE SCENES. MUSIC AND CODE ARE EQUAL PARTS OF THE JOB, AND HE’S FLUENT IN BOTH.

Andres Sanchis
Product Developer
ANDRES@CASSETTEMUSIC.COM

ANDRES BUILDS THE BACKBONE OF CASSETTE’S TECH. WITH A MASTER’S IN SOFTWARE ENGINEERING AND YEARS OF EXPERIENCE ACROSS EVERYTHING FROM APP DEVELOPMENT TO FULL-SCALE SYSTEMS, HE KEEPS US RUNNING SMOOTHLY AND SOUNDING SHARP. ANDROID, IOS, BACKEND, FRONTEND—WHATEVER NEEDS BUILDING, HE’S ON IT.

‍

BOARD
Stephen Hanson
Investor & Advisor

STEPHEN HANSON HAS SPENT HIS CAREER SHAPING THE WAY PEOPLE EXPERIENCE HOSPITALITY. AS THE FOUNDER OF BR GUEST HOSPITALITY, HE BUILT 42 RESTAURANTS AND FOUR INDEPENDENT HOTEL BRANDS ACROSS NYC, VEGAS, CHICAGO, AND BEYOND—LAUNCHING INDUSTRY STAPLES LIKE BLUE WATER GRILL, DOS CAMINOS, AND THE AWARD-WINNING FIAMMA WHILE MENTORING MICHELIN-STARRED CHEFS ALONG THE WAY. A NATURAL AT KNOWING WHAT PEOPLE WANT BEFORE THEY DO, HANSON’S INSTINCT FOR HOSPITALITY STARTED IN THE ‘70S AT TGI FRIDAY’S, AND TURNED INTO A 27-YEAR RUN AT BR GUEST, LATER ACQUIRED BY STARWOOD CAPITAL GROUP. NOW, HE BRINGS THAT SAME SHARP SENSE OF ATMOSPHERE AND EXPERIENCE TO CASSETTE—BECAUSE GREAT SPACES DON’T JUST LOOK THE PART. THEY SHOULD SOUND UNFORGETTABLE, TOO.

Paul Blair
Grammy Award Winning DJ

DJ WHITE SHADOW (PAUL BLAIR) IS A CLIO AND GRAMMY-WINNING PRODUCER, DJ, AND CREATIVE VISIONARY KNOWN FOR SHAPING THE SOUND OF MODERN POP AND ELECTRONIC MUSIC. BEST RECOGNIZED FOR HIS WORK AS LADY GAGA’S KEY COLLABORATOR ON BORN THIS WAY AND ARTPOP, HE HAS CRAFTED CHART-TOPPING

- Condensed body copy in monospace font
- High-contrast photography of audio equipment
- Brand partner logos in monochrome
- Geometric shapes and abstract elements
- Interactive audio interface elements

- Strong use of black as primary background
- Bright yellow as primary accent color
- Blue, pink, and green as secondary accents
- Geometric patterns and lines
- Grain textures adding depth
- Minimal use of borders, emphasis on color blocks
- Technical/audio-inspired graphics

The design successfully balances technical precision with creative expression, reflecting the audio industry's dual nature of technical expertise and artistic creativity.

Travel

Bourdain’s been on my mind again. Not just the food, the knives, the back-of-the-kitchen grit — but how he moved through the world. Curious. Sharp. Honest. Flawed in a way that made sense. I miss that kind of honesty.

My Sourdough Bread

My Sourdough Bread

500g Bread Flour

350g Lukewarm Water

100g Active Sourdough Starter

10g Of Salt

Start in the morning. Grab a big bowl and toss in 500g of bread flour (or all-purpose, don’t overthink it) with 350g of lukewarm water. Stir it into a shaggy mess. You’re not kneading, just mixing. Cover the bowl and walk away for 30–45 minutes. Let the flour and water get to know each other, this is the autolyse, where the magic begins.

Come back. Now, add 100g of your bubbly sourdough starter (you fed it last night, right?) and 10g of salt. Use your hands and mix it all together. It’ll be sticky, borderline annoying, but just keep folding and squishing until it feels more like a dough than a swamp.

FDIC Insurance
Current is a financial technology company, not an FDIC-insured bank. FDIC insurance only covers the failure of an FDIC-insured bank. FDIC insurance up to $250,000 is available on customer funds through pass-through insurance at Choice Financial Group, Member FDIC, and Cross River Bank, Member FDIC where we have a direct relationship for the placement of deposits and into which customer funds are deposited, but only if certain conditions have been met. There may be a risk that FDIC insurance is not available because conditions have not been satisfied. In such cases, funds may not be fully insured in the event the insured depository institution where the funds have been deposited were to fail.

Faster Direct Deposit

4 Steps To Take Complete Control of Your Debt
Scan Your Records

We search nationwide databases to find any outstanding debt in your name.

Download iOS app
Review Your Matches

We’ll send you a comprehensive report listing your specific debts, verified collector names, and exactly what you owe.

Let AI Negotiate for You

Our AI assistant will negotiate on your behalf, creating the best possible terms for resolving your debt.

Monitor Your Progress

Enjoy peace of mind with real-time updates tracking the progress of your accounts.

Download iOS app
Frequently Asked Questions

Our all-in-one platform empowers you to take control of your financial future with powerful tools that simplify the debt resolution process.

What does Debtfindr do?

Debtfindr helps you discover debts tied to your name, negotiate settlements using AI, and resolve them—all in one secure app.

How do you find debts in my name?

We work with verified data providers and partner agencies to scan for collections accounts associated with your identity—securely and privately.

Will checking for debt hurt my credit score?

No. Scanning for debts with Debtfindr is a soft check and does not affect your credit score.

What happens if you don’t find any debt?

Great news! We’ll keep scanning daily and notify you if anything new appears—so you’re always one step ahead.

What if I do have debt?

You’ll see exactly who the collector is, what’s owed, and what your options are. You can then use our built-in AI Assistant to negotiate a lower settlement or set up a payment plan.

Do I have to talk to a debt collector?

Nope. Our AI handles the conversation for you—no phone calls, no awkward negotiations.

Is my information secure?

Absolutely. We use end-to-end encryption and never share your data without your explicit consent.

Can I resolve the debt inside the app?

Yes. If the collector is a verified partner, you can settle your debt directly through the app and receive confirmation instantly.

What does it cost?

Debtfindr is free to try for 14 days. After that, we offer affordable subscription plans for ongoing monitoring and resolution features.

Why should I use Debtfindr instead of a credit monitoring app?

Most credit apps just show what’s already on your report. Debtf

Bottom Section:
- Dark-themed showcase of app functionality
- Multiple mobile screen captures showing the application interface
- Interactive elements and form components displayed in context
- Strong contrast between dark backgrounds and bright UI elements

The design employs consistent spacing with generous padding between sections. UI components feature subtle shadows and rounded corners. The visual hierarchy emphasizes the app's functionality through strategically placed screenshots and illustrations.
description.style: Modern minimalist with a bold, tech-forward aesthetic. Combines flat design with subtle depth through shadows and overlays. The style balances playfulness (through illustrations) with professional credibility (through UI demonstrations).
description.audience: Millennials and Gen Z individuals dealing with debt management, specifically targeting tech-savvy users comfortable with mobile-first solutions.
description.layoutStyle: Modern and conventional single-page scroll with creative elements in the presentation of app features and benefits.
description.intent:
The page aims to:
- Introduce an AI-powered debt resolution solution
- Build trust through transparency of the app's functionality
- Drive app downloads/signups
- Educate users about the debt resolution process
- Position the product as a modern, tech-forward solution to debt management

The design emphasizes simplicity and accessibility while maintaining a professional appearance that instills confidence in handling sensitive financial matters. The bright color scheme and friendly illustrations help reduce the anxiety typically associated with debt-related services.

h1s: A guide to digital design at the Guardian
metadata.textContent:
A guide to digital design at the Guardian
26/07/18
Introduction
The Guardian website is made up of a series of fronts and articles.
Fronts are made up of containers — these allow stories to be grouped in a logical manner, by theme or subject matter.
Containers are composed of modular story cards. These are designed to be flexible; they expand and contract depending on the number of stories added to them.
Cards can display an image, kicker, headline, standfirst and meta information.
Kicker — Guardian Headline Bold Headline — Guardian Headline Medium Standfirst — Guardian Text Egyptian Meta — Guardian Text Sans
We have a variety of different card sizes which create hierarchy and pace on the page, and allow the reader to quickly gauge a story’s importance.
This is a selection of the card types. Each card type is designed to impart the tone of the story.
Below is a selection of the article types. Articles follow the design language of the cards, with each having its own distinct flavour and tone.
Thrashers are custom-designed containers usually promoting a single article or series.
Grids &spacing
The grid provides the substrate and unifies how we arrange information on all of our digital platforms.
The desktop grid is based on columns of 60px with 20px gutters.
We use four fixed grid sizes on large screens:
Wide — 1300px LeftCol — 1140px Desktop — 980px Tablet — 740px
We use a fluid grid on mobile devices, with tweak points at:
Phablet — 740px MobileLandscape — 480px MobileMedium — 375px
Spacing is intentionally used to echo the placement of the typography in print.
Rules govern the placement of display and text elements and maintain consistency across print and digital platforms.
This alignment is consistent on headlines, bylines and container titles across fronts and articles.
Brand use
The Guardian has two expressions of its brand.
The logo and the roundel are always aligned either top right or bottom right.
The roundel acts as a simplified expression of the brand — often used to indicate Guardian content on- and off-platform. For more, visit The Guardian Brand hub
Typography
The Guardian has two core typefaces and three additional typefaces.
Guardian Headline (core) Guardian Titlepiece (core) Guardian Text Egyptian Guardian Display Sans Guardian Text Sans
Guardian Headline is designed to provide an optimal reading experience across all platforms. It is used on the website and app for headlines, providing their unique character and voice.
Guardian Titlepiece was drawn from the Guardian’s logotype and is used for section titling and signposting. High contrast, bold and striking — use cautiously and appropriately!
Highly legible text type complements our display family.
We use our sans serif for small sizes or meta information.
Colour palette
Our colour palette allows us to be bold, playful, tasteful and sensitive.
We use colour also as a navigational aid for our readers, therefore consistency is crucial.
Colour is an important tool, both for our UI and for signifying the type of editorial content and the pillar under which that content sits.
Each pillar’s colour group comprises of a dark, main, bright, pastel and faded colour variant. Showing:
In addition to our pillar colours, we use ‘Highlight’ for important information, an extensive array of ‘Neutrals’ for page architecture and a set of template specific colours. Showing:
Graphic elements
Various devices create hierarchy, structure and help readers to navigate.
We have two styles of button. Consider weighting when choosing a style: the lozenge is bolder, the circular button is more subtle. Icons help the reader navigate and sit centrally inside an imaginary circle at the edge of the button.
We use highlighting for impact and to create hierarchy and emphasis within designs.
Rules provide structure and also add a subtle difference in flavour across article types. We have four styles for different parts of the website.

description.pageDescription:
This appears to be a design system or style guide page for The Guardian's digital properties. The layout employs a clean, minimalist structure with significant whitespace. The page features a bold yellow header banner that spans the full width, containing the text "Thinking outside the boxes" and The Guardian's logo. Below this is what appears to be an introduction or documentation section with carefully structured typography.

The layout follows a clear hierarchical grid system with:
- Wide header banner in bright yellow (#FFE500)
- Left-aligned content area with generous margins
- Systematic vertical spacing between text elements
- Clean typographic hierarchy using The Guardian's custom fonts
- Minimal decoration or embellishment, letting the content breathe

The content appears to be organized into distinct sections covering brand guidelines, typography, color, and graphic elements. The page uses a combination of serif and sans-serif typography that reflects The Guardian's editorial brand identity.
description.style: Modern minimalist with a strong editorial influence. The design embraces whitespace and typography-focused presentation typical of style guides and design documentation. The aesthetic is clean and functional while maintaining The Guardian's distinctive brand character.
description.audience: Internal design teams, developers, and creative professionals who need to implement The Guardian's digital brand guidelines. The technical and detailed nature of the content suggests it's meant for practitioners rather than general audiences.
description.pageType: Design system documentation / Brand guidelines page
description.layoutStyle: Conventional documentation layout optimized for readability and reference, with a creative twist in the header treatment that adds brand personality.
description.intent:
To serve as a comprehensive reference for The Guardian's digital design language and provide clear guidelines for maintaining brand consistency across digital products. The page aims to educate users about proper implementation of The Guardian's design system while demonstrating those principles in its own design.

The stark, documentation-style layout balanced with bold brand elements (like the yellow header) effectively communicates both authority and accessibility - key values for The Guardian's brand identity.

Doconomy partners with financial institutions to drive positive impact on a global scale by offering innovative banking tools supported by human-centric design, behavioral science and superior data.

OUR PRODUCTS

Doconomy’s products unite financial services with climate consciousness. Using innovation, technology, and behavioral science, we bridge the intent-action gap by empowering users to make sustainable financial choices, driving positive impact globally.

OUR APPROACH
ENVIRONMENTAL AND FINANCIAL WELLBEING

Our conviction is that financial wellbeing accelerates climate consciousness. In order to make sustainable choices, individuals need to feel financial control and empowered to make meaningful change.

The Impact finance product has been co-created with leading scientists from the fields of psychology, neuroscience and behavioral economics to deliver radical new ways to boost your customers’ digital engagement, financial- and environmental wellbeing.

Emotions are the single most powerful drivers of human behavior. Tap into your customers’ emotions to activate their intrinsic motivation, trigger sustainable  behavioral change and increase their engagement.​

With the acquisition of Dreams Technology, Doconomy has shown a long-term commitment to innovating and supporting banks to  empower financial wellbeing throughout their customer base.

Sections (top to bottom):

metadata.title: Ontdek Eindhoven Design District | Eindhoven Design District
metadata.description: Eindhoven Design District is een initiatief van de gemeente Eindhoen, DDF en EHV365 om ontwerpkracht te stimuleren en design zichtbaarder te maken.
metadata.ogDescription: Eindhoven Design District is een initiatief van de gemeente Eindhoen, DDF en EHV365 om ontwerpkracht te stimuleren en design zichtbaarder te maken.
h2s: Wil je weten hoe jij ontwerpkracht inzet voor jouw project?
metadata.textContent:
NL
EN
search
Menu
Ontdek design in Eindhoven

Eindhoven Design District is een initiatief van gemeente Eindhoven, Dutch Design Foundation en Eindhoven365 om design en technologie in Eindhoven een groter podium te bieden en design thinking te stimuleren bij bedrijven en overheden. Op deze website vind je informatie over de projecten die wij realiseren en best-practices uit het Eindhovense ontwerpveld.

Meer over Eindhoven Design District
Projecten
Infomarkers Eindhoven Design District

Eindhoven is dé stad van technologie, kennis en design. Maar dat laatste wordt vooral duidelijk tijdens de Dutch Design Week. Zonde, want design vind je niet alleen achter ateliermuren, maar ook in de openbare ruimte. Een aantrekkelijke ‘familie’ van informatiemarkers maakt design in Eindhoven nog beter zichtbaar en beleefbaar. Kom dat zien, het hele jaar door.

Lees meer
Lees meer
Erfgoedputdeksels

Sinds 2024 heeft de binnenstad van Eindhoven unieke putdeksels. De deksels – ontworpen door Atelier van Asseldonk – verwijzen subtiel naar de naoorlogse architectuur in de stad. De putdeksels vertellen zo het verhaal van Eindhovens erfgoed dat waarschijnlijk niet zo bekend is. Het ontwerp nodigt bewoners en bezoekers van het centrum uit om met een andere blik te kijken naar de stad om hen heen.

Lees meer
Lees meer
Pretty Plastic

In 2016 presenteerde start-up Pretty Plastic tijdens Dutch Design Week zijn eerste plastic geveltegel. Wat begon vanuit frustratie over de grote hoeveelheid plastic afval, vind je nu op gevels van gebouwen door heel Europa én op het Franse eiland Mayotte. Inmiddels produceert Pretty Plastic de tegels met tweedehands pvc – een zeer moeilijk te recyclen materiaal. Ook op de gevel van het vernieuwde zwembad de Tongelreep in Eindhoven vind je ze terug. Monteurs schroefden hier ruim dertigduizend Pretty Plastic-tegels vast.

Lees meer
Lees meer
In de volle grond

Eindhoven is vol. En dat geldt niet alleen boven de grond, maar ook eronder. Daar ligt namelijk een wirwar aan kabels, leidingen, parkeergarages en ondergrondse containers. Het is zó vol, dat er eigenlijk niets meer bij kan. Een interactieve expo tijdens Dutch Design Week 2022 maakte geïnteresseerden, bewoners én professionals in ondergronds werk hiervan bewust. Verdiepende workshops zorgden bij die laatste groep voor verantwoordelijkheidsbesef én concrete oplossingrichtingen.

Lees meer
Lees meer
Alle projecten
Artikelen
Interview
Maak ruimte voor creativiteit - over het belang van broedplaatsen voor Eindhoven

De ruimte én creatieve maakkracht op Sectie-C staan onder druk, want in een stad waar de woningnood hoog is, moet elke vierkante meter zo nuttig mogelijk gebruikt worden. Jur Jacobs en Mats Horbach vertellen over de unieke uitdagingen die komen kijken bij de ontwikkeling van het gebied en geven hun kijk op de creatieve maaksector in de stad.

Interview
Een brug slaan tussen design en stedelijke transformatie

Een van Eindhovens paradepaardjes is Design Academy Eindhoven (DAE). Hoe ziet deze academie met internationale allure haar rol in lokale ontwikkelingen? En hoe verbinden we jong ontwerptalent aan de stad? We vroegen het Raf De Keninck, directeur onderwijs, onderzoek en organisatie bij DAE.

Interview
Design thinking bij Bibliotheek Eindhoven

Wie denkt aan design thinking en innovatie, denkt waarschijnlijk niet zo snel aan een bibliotheek. Toch is Bibliotheek Eindhoven hierin een van de koplopers van de stad, of zelfs van het land. Directeur Albert Kivits vertelt over ontwerpgedreven organisatieveranderingen en innoveren binnen de bieb.

Interview
Ontwerpen met verantwoordelijkheid

Dat designtalent in Eindhoven blijft plakken, bewijst de in Polen geboren Zuzanna Skalska, eigenaar en oprichter van het succesvolle adviesbureau 360inspiration. Welke rol hebben ontwerpers volgens haar in onze veranderende maatschappij? En hoe vertaalt die rol zich naar een stad als Eindhoven?

Interview
Innovatie: “De echte winst zit in het begrijpen van de keten”

Iemand die alles kan vertellen over de innovatiekracht van Eindhoven is Paul Gardien, tot voor kort vicepresident bij Philips Design en tegenwoordig fellow bij TU/e. Waarom zijn ontwerpers volgens hem nodig in ieder bedrijf? En wat is zijn visie op de toekomst van de ontwerper?

Zie alle artikelen
Ontdek design in Eindhoven

Op This is Eindhoven vind je informatie, routes en achtergrondverhalen over design in de stad.

Visit Thisiseindhoven.com
Ontdek design met de Designroute Strijp

Of je nou een doorgewinterde designliefhebber bent, of gewoon eens kennis wilt maken met de ontwerpkracht van Eindhoven Design District: deze route is voor jou.

Designroute Strijp
Wat kun je met speculatief ontwerp?

In deze aflevering van podcast De Tekentafel spreken we drie ontwerpers die zich bezighouden met speculatief ontwerp: Frank Kolkman, Merle Bergers en Marleen van Bergeijk.

Listen to the podcast
7x Design areas & broedplaatsen

description.pageDescription:
The page follows a clean, grid-based layout with distinct content sections. The top features a minimalist header with simple navigation. The main content area is organized into a "Projecten" (Projects) section that uses a striking color-blocked design approach:

Layout Structure:
- Two-column layout alternating between colored blocks and project images
- Strong vertical rhythm with consistent spacing
- Each project block spans full-width with a 50/50 split between text and image

Sections:
1. Header with brief Dutch text introduction
2. Projects showcase featuring:
   - Pink block with "Infomarkers Eindhoven Design District" paired with wayfinding sign photo
   - Red block with "Erfgoedputdeksels" paired with decorative manhole cover image
   - Blue block with "Pretty Plastic" paired with textured material photo
   - Pink block with "In de volle grond" paired with soil/nature photo
3. "Artikelen" (Articles) section at bottom with three article cards showing thumbnail images

Visual Elements:
- Bold color blocks in pink, red, and blue
- High-contrast typography against colored backgrounds
- Documentary-style photography
- Generous whitespace
- Clean, sans-serif typography
- Minimal decorative elements

Interactions:
- Subtle hover states on clickable elements
- Simple navigation system
- Article cards appear clickable
description.style: Modern minimalist with bold color blocking and strong geometric elements
description.audience: Design professionals, architects, urban planners, and those interested in Dutch design and urban development
description.layoutStyle: Contemporary with experimental color blocking, breaking from conventional web layouts while maintaining clear hierarchy and readability
description.intent:
To showcase design initiatives and projects in Eindhoven's design district, positioning the area as a hub for design and innovation. The page aims to inform visitors about various design projects and encourage exploration of the district's design initiatives.

The design effectively balances bold visual statements with clear information architecture, creating an engaging yet professional presentation of design-focused content. The use of strong colors and documentary photography creates a distinctive visual identity that reflects the innovative nature of the design district.

description.pageDescription:
The page showcases a modern product design platform called Surf through a series of mobile app and desktop interface mockups arranged in a vertical scrolling layout. The design uses a dark theme with vibrant gradient backgrounds combining deep teals, purples, and greens. The layout is organized into several key sections:

The header features a clean, minimalist logo "Surf" with a geometric wave pattern. Below this are multiple showcase sections displaying various UI components and screens from what appears to be a financial or trading platform interface. The mockups show detailed charts, transaction lists, and user interface elements in a dark mode aesthetic.

The interface examples demonstrate sophisticated data visualization with graphs, statistics, and interactive elements. Each section is thoughtfully spaced with consistent padding and alignment, creating a clear visual hierarchy. The design makes extensive use of rounded corners, subtle shadows, and glass-morphic effects that give depth to the interface elements.

Mobile app screenshots are arranged in groups of three, showing different states and features of the application. The spacing between elements is generous, allowing each component to breathe while maintaining visual connection.
description.style: Ultra-modern with a blend of glassmorphism and dark mode aesthetics. The design language is sophisticated and technical, incorporating contemporary UI trends like gradient overlays, transparent elements, and smooth transitions.
description.audience: Startup founders, product designers, and tech entrepreneurs looking for high-end design solutions for their digital products, particularly in the fintech or trading space.
description.layoutStyle: Contemporary and experimental, utilizing asymmetric layouts and dynamic spacing while maintaining professional polish and clarity.
description.intent:
The page aims to demonstrate Surf's design capabilities and attract potential clients by showcasing sophisticated interface design work. It positions the company as a premium design partner for ambitious tech startups and founders, particularly those building complex digital products.

The combination of professional aesthetics and technical sophistication suggests this is targeting founders who value high-quality design as a crucial component of their product strategy. The page encourages viewers to engage with the studio for their design needs through subtle but clear calls-to-action integrated throughout the showcase.

description.pageDescription:
The page follows a sophisticated, modern layout with distinctive characteristics:

Layout Structure:
- Asymmetric grid system with generous whitespace
- Strong horizontal sections with dynamic spacing
- Bold typography-driven design with clear visual hierarchy
- Orange geometric shapes and accents provide visual punctuation

Sections:
1. Header with minimalist navigation and striking orange geometric shape
2. Hero section featuring "FABRIC" in large, bold typography with a distinctive strikethrough element
3. Portfolio grid showcasing partner logos (Polkadot, Near, BlackPool, etc.) in a clean, organized matrix
4. Content blocks with "IMMUNITY FIRST - RESEARCH DRIVEN - FOUNDER LED - COMM" repeated as section dividers
5. Interactive team section with geometric orange pattern elements
6. Writing/blog section with article cards and orange square pattern accents

Visual Elements:
- Primary color palette: Black, white, and vibrant orange (#FF5500)
- Geometric patterns and shapes, particularly circular/semi-circular forms
- Clean lines and bold typography
- Minimal use of imagery except for logos and essential graphics
- Strong use of negative space
- Angular geometric patterns as decorative elements
description.style: Modern brutalist with a tech-forward aesthetic, combining minimalism with bold geometric elements and strong typography
description.audience: Tech founders, blockchain/Web3 entrepreneurs, cryptocurrency innovators, and technology investors
description.pageType: Homepage/Landing page for a venture capital firm
description.layoutStyle: Experimental/Creative - breaking conventional layout rules while maintaining professional polish
description.intent:
- Primary: Attract founders seeking investment and showcase the firm's Web3/crypto focus
- Secondary: Display portfolio companies and establish credibility in the blockchain/crypto space
- Call-to-action: Drive applications for their residency accelerator program

The design effectively communicates a forward-thinking, tech-savvy investment firm through its bold typographic choices and geometric visual language. The orange accent color and striking patterns create memorable branded moments throughout the scroll experience.

2024/25 — A Year of Impact

As we enter our seventh year as a business, we’re reflecting on 2024. It was quite the year for us.

CASE STUDY
Thinking Differently with a rebrand for ADHD Foundation

ADHD Foundation’s digital presence and brand identity hadn’t quite kept up with their ground-breaking work and ever-expanding mission. Until now, that is.

A client’s view of the Nurture Programme

What started as a pilot in 2023 has grown into a cracking annual fundraiser that's now raised over £20,000 across just 9 total days of streaming.

description.pageDescription:
The website employs a sophisticated dark theme layout with a strong emphasis on vertical scrolling and clear content hierarchy. The structure includes:

- Header section with minimalist navigation and logo
- Hero section featuring a clean, bold headline against a dark background
- Statistics/impact section showing key numbers (250+, 45k) with supporting text
- Portfolio/work showcase section featuring circular images with bright blue accents
- Photography sections showing outdoor scenes and activities
- Interactive elements embedded within a single-column layout
- Generous whitespace between sections creating clear content separation

The visual hierarchy is established through:
- Large, bold typography using PP Neue Montreal
- High contrast between dark backgrounds and light text
- Strategic use of bright blue accent colors
- Circular image masks creating visual interest
- Consistent vertical rhythm and spacing
- Clean grid system with full-width sections
description.style: Modern minimalist with a premium feel. The design embraces contemporary web trends while maintaining sophistication through careful use of negative space and typography. The dark theme adds gravitas and professional polish.
description.audience: Non-profit organizations and charities seeking professional digital services. The design speaks to organizations that value modern, professional presentation while maintaining approachability and trust.
description.pageType: Homepage/Landing Page serving as main entry point and overview of services
description.layoutStyle: Contemporary single-page scroll layout that follows modern web design conventions while introducing subtle creative elements like circular image masks and careful typography scaling.
description.intent:
The page aims to:
- Establish credibility in the charity/non-profit sector
- Showcase impact through statistics and case studies
- Present a professional yet approachable image
- Generate inquiries from charitable organizations
- Communicate values alignment with social impact sector

The design effectively balances professional sophistication with warmth and accessibility, appropriate for organizations focused on social good. The circular motif throughout creates visual consistency while the dark theme adds gravitas to the social impact message.

The page employs a modern, enterprise-focused layout with a clear visual hierarchy. The structure includes:

- Header section with minimal navigation and prominent "Get in touch" CTA
- Hero section featuring a bold headline "Make your retail stores even more profitable" with supporting visuals showing retail merchandising interfaces
- Product feature grid showcasing key benefits with performance statistics (+34% indicators)
- Social proof section displaying logos of major retail brands (Venroy, Assembly Label, etc.)

- Primary: Deep purple (#2D1C59)
- Secondary: Soft peach (#FFE9E1)

- Interface screenshots showing the product in use

Personalised Aviation
More flexibility, freedom and choice!
All SolutionsOwnership
We believe that in a world where passengers have become numbers, a personal approach is key to ensure you get the most out of your flying experience.
Flexible Solutions

Stop depending on airlines. Fly on your own terms or join our exclusive shared flights.

Smart Quotes

Know what you are paying for. No hidden fees. No overpromises. Flights suitable for your needs.

Fully Customizable

Flight Manager

All of our flights come with complimentary Flight Manager Service. Your dedicated Flight Manager ensures that every detail of your flight is taken care of and you get the most competitive tailor-made offers. 
Speak with your Flight Manager in English 🇬🇧, Dutch 🇳🇱, German 🇩🇪, Spanish 🇪🇸 or Portuguese 🇵🇹. 

Hyer® Experience

At Hyer® we are bold. The aircraft is just a tool we use to get you where you want to be. Our clients enjoy much more value for their investment, accessing unparalleled personalisation when flying private.

We offer personalised experiences with our on-demand charters and fractional ownership. We upgrade the way you fly.

Rewarding Membership

Our membership blends the advantage of a jet card with the easiness of a loyalty program. Get access to services not available anywhere without hefty fees.

Aircraft ownership

Our team is ready to assist in discovering if ownership of an aircraft could be something for you. Let us help you with a shared ownership construction together with like-minded clients. 

CO2 Neutral Flights

We play our part to ensure a more sustainable future. Via Hyer® CO2 offset program, clients can offset the entire emission of their flights.

Digital Care

We don't do business as if it was the 80's. Receive instant estimates online. Get a quote with one click. Book your aircraft via a seamless process.

Service Add-ons

Avoid hidden fees and pay only for the service that you need. Upgrade your flight with multiple add-ons. Shape your trip to meet your requirements.

Personal Flight Manager

Our experts can support any trip and request, 24/07. From short business trips to large groups crossing the globe for leisure. Enjoy a stress-free booking process.

description.pageDescription:
The page follows a modern, asymmetrical grid layout with generous whitespace. The design begins with a clean header featuring the Flyer logo and minimal navigation. The hero section presents a bold "Personalised Aviation" heading alongside a sleek private jet image, establishing luxury positioning.

The layout flows through distinct sections:
- Hero with large typography and aircraft imagery
- Value proposition statements about personalized service
- Service categories displayed in a horizontal scroll featuring "On-Demand" and "Ownership" with accompanying aircraft images
- Popular destinations grid showing cities like Barcelona, Ibiza, Cannes, and Paris with destination codes
- Flight Manager product showcase with mobile app interface demonstrations
- Experience/membership section with clean iconography and feature lists

Visual Elements:
- Color palette: Dark navy, white, warm terracotta/copper tones
- High-contrast photography of luxury aircraft
- Sophisticated typography using HelveticaNowDisplay
- Minimal UI elements with subtle shadows
- Clean lines and geometric shapes
- Professional aviation imagery
- Careful use of negative space

Interactions:
- Subtle hover states on navigation items
- Horizontal scrolling content sections
- Interactive destination selection
- Mobile app interface demonstrations
- Clear call-to-action buttons
description.style: Modern luxury minimalism with a focus on sophistication and premium positioning. The design employs contemporary web practices while maintaining an exclusive feel.
description.audience: High-net-worth individuals, business executives, and luxury travelers seeking private aviation services. The design speaks to a sophisticated audience that values personalization and premium experiences.
description.pageType: Homepage/Landing Page hybrid that introduces the brand while driving conversion
description.layoutStyle: Contemporary asymmetrical layout that follows modern web design trends while maintaining professional sophistication
description.intent:
The page aims to:
- Position Flyer as a premium, personalized aviation service
- Showcase the convenience and luxury of private aviation
- Drive sign-ups for the Flight Manager service
- Convert high-value clients into either on-demand bookings or ownership programs
- Communicate trustworthiness and exclusivity through refined design

The design successfully balances luxury positioning with functional user experience, making private aviation feel both aspirational and accessible to the target audience.

Neu im Team: Theresa Götz

Welcome to the team!

Seit Juli 2025 ist Theresa im FORA-Team im Bereich Projektmanagement, Kund:innen-Beratung und Konzeption tätig. Wir freuen uns total, da sie nicht nur fachlich eine super gute Ergänzung zum Team ist sondern vor allem sehr viel Motivation und Freude mitbringt. Das wundert uns nicht, denn Theresa ist zusätzlich als Sport Coach und Trainerin unterwegs, also ist sie es gewohnt Menschen zu ihren Höchstleistungen zu bringen. Kurz gesagt: Therese bewegt sich und andere – am besten in der Gruppe. Das passt perfekt zu FORA und unserem Ansatz Menschen zusammen zu bringen und gemeinsam Mehrwerte zu schaffen. Neben viel Bewegung und ihrer Begeisterung für Kreativität, reist Theresa wahnsinnig gern durch die Welt und bringt uns dann gute Stories mit ins Büro.

Schön, dass du Teil des FORA-Teams bist, Theresa!

description.pageDescription:
The page follows a modern, asymmetrical layout with three main columns:

Left Column:
- Large "FORA" logotype in black serif typography
- Simple "Say hi!" text with an underlined link
- Company name footer

Middle Column:
- Purple-tinted newsfeed section
- Three chronological posts with dates (Sep 5, Jan 7, Jun 3)
- Each post has a thumbnail image grid and "Mehr lesen" (Read more) link
- Hashtag illustration at bottom

Right Column:
- Main content area featuring "Neu im Team" announcement
- Vibrant yellow-green background
- Mood board-style image collage including:
  - Lemons with leaves
  - Luxury pool/architecture shot
  - High-end bicycle
  - Pastry/coffee imagery
  - Travel/landscape photographs
- German text description
- Clean typography with ample whitespace

The layout uses sophisticated spacing and alignment, with careful attention to visual hierarchy. Navigation is minimal and elegant, with the main menu items (About, Team, Work, Contact) in the footer.
description.style: Contemporary European minimalist with a warm, sophisticated edge. Combines corporate professionalism with creative playfulness through the use of lifestyle imagery and bold color blocking.
description.audience: Creative professionals and corporate clients interested in strategy and communications services. The bilingual German/English content and high-end imagery suggests a focus on sophisticated international clientele.
description.pageType: Team announcement/news page within a corporate website
description.layoutStyle: Modern experimental layout that breaks traditional grid conventions while maintaining clear visual hierarchy and usability.
description.intent:
The page serves multiple purposes:
1. Announce new team member addition
2. Showcase company culture through curated imagery
3. Demonstrate design sophistication to potential clients
4. Maintain company news/updates
5. Provide easy navigation to core site sections

The design successfully balances professional credibility with creative innovation, positioning FORA as a modern, sophisticated agency that values both strategy and aesthetics.

description.pageDescription:
The page follows a clean, minimal grid layout with significant whitespace. The structure includes:

- Header: Minimal top navigation with logo "LA CA 14.02.13" and "Build with us" link
- Hero Section: Large typography presenting the main value proposition split across the width
- Portfolio Grid: A masonry-style layout displaying project work through high-quality photographs and mockups, featuring editorial-style brand imagery, product photography, and print design
- Client Logos: Minimalist monochrome logos including Nike and other prestigious brands
- Projects Section: Features large, dynamic project thumbnails with hover states showing project titles and categories

The visual language employs:
- Monochromatic color palette dominated by blacks, whites, and grays
- Sharp typography using Inter and Everett Web fonts
- Generous negative space
- High-contrast imagery
- Subtle transitions and hover effects
- Clean lines and geometric shapes
- Professional photography and mockups
description.style: Contemporary minimalist with a high-end editorial aesthetic. The design embraces modernist principles with a focus on typography and negative space.
description.audience: Creative professionals and established businesses seeking sophisticated brand development and digital creative services. Appeals to design-conscious clients who value premium aesthetic and contemporary brand expression.
description.pageType: Portfolio homepage showcasing agency work and capabilities
description.layoutStyle: Modern and experimental while maintaining clear hierarchy and usability. The layout breaks conventional patterns while remaining intuitive.
description.intent:
To position the studio as a premium creative partner for ambitious brands, demonstrate their caliber of work through careful curation, and encourage potential clients to initiate contact. The page aims to convey sophisticated taste level and strategic capability through its restrained design approach.

The site effectively balances artistic expression with commercial appeal, using carefully considered typography and spacing to create an atmosphere of expertise and exclusivity.

description.pageDescription:
The page employs a sophisticated editorial-style layout with a clear visual hierarchy. The structure includes:

- Header section with minimal navigation and clean typography
- A hero section featuring high-end architectural photography with ample whitespace
- A grid-based gallery showcasing luxury residential projects through large-format images
- Interior design shots highlighting modern, minimalist spaces with neutral color palettes
- Lower sections featuring architectural and interior photography organized in an asymmetric grid

The layout demonstrates careful attention to spacing and proportion, using generous whitespace and precise alignment. Content is organized in a modular grid system that allows photography to breathe while maintaining visual rhythm. Key sections include:

- Project showcase grid with full-width architectural imagery
- Interior spaces featuring floor-to-ceiling windows and contemporary furnishings
- Professional architectural photography emphasizing clean lines and modern design
- Text overlays using refined typography in Inter and Canela fonts
- Subtle quotations and statements integrated into the layout

Visual elements follow a restrained palette:
- Monochromatic grays and whites
- Rich architectural photography
- Minimal use of decorative elements
- Emphasis on natural light and shadows in imagery
- Clean lines and geometric shapes
description.style: Minimalist luxury, architectural sophistication, modern elegance
description.audience: High-end property buyers, luxury real estate investors, design-conscious professionals interested in premium residential properties
description.pageType: Corporate homepage for luxury property development company
description.layoutStyle: Contemporary editorial with architectural influence, following modern luxury web design conventions while maintaining sophistication
description.intent:
- Establish brand positioning in luxury property development
- Showcase portfolio of high-end residential projects
- Communicate design expertise and attention to detail
- Appeal to sophisticated buyers seeking premium properties
- Build trust through professional presentation and quality imagery

The design successfully conveys exclusivity and architectural excellence through its refined visual language and careful attention to detail in both layout and content presentation.

description.pageDescription:
The page features a modern, airy layout with a distinctive split-screen design. The top section utilizes a light, minimal color scheme with soft pastels - primarily white, light blue, and coral pink accents. The layout employs a sophisticated grid system with generous whitespace and strategic spacing.

Key sections include:
1. Header with minimal navigation and dark mode toggle
2. Hero section with large typography and abstract geometric shapes floating in the background
3. Feature showcase area with a horizontal scroll of project thumbnails/cards
4. Interactive elements highlighted by subtle hover animations
5. Bottom gallery/portfolio strip showing various design examples in a horizontal scroll

The visual hierarchy is well-defined through typography scale and spatial relationships. Content is organized in a clean, scannable way with clear separation between sections. The design incorporates floating geometric elements and subtle gradients that create depth without overwhelming the content.

Interactive elements feature smooth transitions and hover effects. The navigation is minimalist yet functional, with clear CTAs. The imagery consists of both product screenshots and decorative elements that maintain visual consistency through a cohesive color palette.
description.style: Modern minimalist with a touch of glassmorphism. The design embraces contemporary web trends while maintaining clarity and purpose. The aesthetic is clean and sophisticated with playful geometric accents.
description.audience: Design professionals, creative teams, and digital product designers who need collaborative design tools and workflows.
description.layoutStyle: Modern and experimental, pushing beyond conventional patterns while maintaining usability. The floating elements and asymmetrical balance create visual interest.
description.intent:
The page aims to showcase a design collaboration platform, encouraging teams to sign up and start using the tool. The primary goal appears to be demonstrating how the platform can streamline design workflows and team collaboration. The messaging emphasizes efficiency and ease of use for design teams.

The use of Geist and Aeonik fonts reinforces the modern, technical nature of the product while maintaining readability. The typography system creates clear hierarchies through size and weight variations, helping users navigate the content effectively.

The overall design successfully balances aesthetic appeal with functional clarity, using contemporary design patterns to create an engaging yet professional experience that speaks to its target audience of design professionals.

description.pageDescription:
The GitHub homepage features a sophisticated dark mode design with a deep navy/black background and striking purple/blue accent lighting effects. The layout follows a vertical scrolling structure with several distinct sections:

- Header: Clean navigation bar with logo and primary menu items
- Hero section: Features the tagline "The future of building happens together" with ethereal cloud graphics
- Search functionality: Prominent search bar with multiple filter options
- Features sections: Multiple card-based layouts showcasing different GitHub capabilities:
  - Workflow acceleration tools
  - Built-in security features
  - Collaboration tools
  - Code management interfaces

The design employs extensive use of glowing effects and gradient overlays, creating a premium, futuristic atmosphere. Content blocks are arranged in a clear visual hierarchy with ample whitespace. Interactive elements feature subtle hover states and smooth transitions.

Key visual elements include:
- Floating UI panels with code snippets
- Security shield icons
- Stat displays showing "70% MTTR reduction" and "8.3M secret leaks stopped"
- Subtle grid patterns in the background
- Frosted glass effect on cards and panels
- Carefully crafted shadows and lighting effects
description.style: Modern, futuristic with strong cyberpunk influences. Combines glassmorphism with dramatic lighting effects and a dark theme. Premium, sophisticated aesthetic that emphasizes technical capability.
description.audience: Professional developers, software engineers, development teams, and technical decision-makers in software organizations.
description.layoutStyle: Modern and experimental, pushing beyond conventional patterns while maintaining clear information hierarchy and usability.
description.intent:
- Primary: Showcase GitHub as the premier platform for collaborative software development
- Secondary: Drive sign-ups and platform adoption
- Demonstrate advanced technical capabilities while maintaining accessibility
- Position GitHub as the future of software development collaboration

The design successfully balances sophisticated visual effects with clear communication of product value propositions, creating an immersive experience that appeals to technical professionals while maintaining functionality.
