description.pageDescription:
This is a minimal, typography-driven design with a strong emphasis on whitespace and vertical rhythm. The layout follows a single-column structure that expands into a grid gallery towards the bottom.

Layout breakdown:
- Header features a condensed navigation system
- Main content area uses large, bold typography for headlines
- Content is organized in vertical sections with generous spacing
- Project showcase sections alternate between text and images
- Bottom section transitions into a multi-column portfolio grid

The page employs a stark black and white color scheme with occasional pops of neon yellow as an accent. Text is set in Inter, a modern sans-serif typeface that enhances readability and maintains the minimal aesthetic.

Key sections:
1. Hero section with "A DESIGN STUDIO AND VENTURE PARTNER" headline
2. Mission statements in large typography
3. Project showcase with high-contrast images
4. Portfolio grid with hover interactions
5. Footer with additional navigation and contact information

The design uses subtle animations and transitions for interactive elements, with hover states revealing additional information or changing image opacity.
description.style: Contemporary brutalist design with minimalist tendencies. The aesthetic is raw and industrial, featuring stark typography, high contrast, and intentionally simple layouts.
description.audience: Creative professionals, design agencies, startups, and venture capitalists looking for design partnerships. The sophisticated and bold approach suggests a focus on high-level business clients and creative industry leaders.
description.layoutStyle: Experimental/Creative - breaks traditional layout conventions with its typography-first approach and unconventional spacing
description.intent:
The page aims to:
- Establish credibility as a design studio and venture partner
- Showcase previous work and capabilities
- Attract potential clients and partners
- Demonstrate design expertise through the website itself
- Create a memorable, distinctive brand impression

The design effectively communicates sophistication and creative capability while maintaining a clear hierarchy of information and strong visual impact.

description.intent:

The design prioritizes clarity and security over aesthetics, using established patterns that users recognize from similar security checkpoints. The minimal design reduces cognitive load and focuses user attention on the single required action - verifying they are human.

metadata.title: Haptic — The entire history of you
metadata.description: A action-based journal for iPhone
metadata.ogTitle: Haptic — The entire history of you
metadata.ogDescription: A action-based journal for iPhone
h1s: Life is defined by our actions. Everything counts. Books you’ve read, kilometers you’ve run, every cup of coffee, every takeoff and every landing, every workout, every headspace session, every movie, every night out, and every glass of water the next morning. Everything counts. And everything that counts — shapes us
Haptic is a simple and minimalistic action-based journal for iPhone.
Principles of creation. Our core values and beliefs.
We believe that life is defined by actions and memories, not by a collection of facetuned photos.
We aim to build technology that inspires the next steps, instead of focusing on missed opportunities.
Privacy is a fundamental right. Haptic doesn't collect any personal information and doesn't have access to the content that you share in the app.
All your data belongs to you. Every piece of information in the app is encrypted, stored locally on your iPhone and never synced with external servers.
Digital products are tools, not the center of one's life. The app is visible when you need it, and keeps silent when you don't.
Haptic is available for iOS on the AppStore
h2s: Track your habits and activities. All in one timeline.
Get insights based on your actions. See what your life looks like at a glance.
Custom categories to track everything. The only limit is your imagination.
Designed to be private. All added information lives only on your device.

DESIGN, DIFFERENTLY.

We combine our decades of expertise with timeless craftsmanship to streamline your kitchen design process.

JOIN OUR TRADE PROGRAM
TESTIMONIALS

"I've loved getting to know every single woman on the team at Isla Porter. Most cabinet companies have such a limited style vocabulary, which is not only completely uninspiring, but typically makes my job harder and leaves room for more errors. But in this case, the Isla Porter women immediately understood my vision from afar and have worked so hard to bring it to life for me. It's a total style synergy that can't easily be replicated, and saves me so much time and energy in the long run. They make a stressful, taxing process feel fun and inspiring, and their enthusiasm for my project is palpable. I can't wait to use them for client projects next!"

JOURDAN FAIRCHILD, JOURDAN FAIRCHILD DESIGN

"As the kitchen is typically the heart of many homes we work on, we put so much design and effort into the cabinetry and millwork details. Those elements need to be of the best quality made from long-lasting materials and reflect the overall design scheme of the home. Isla Porter has created a platform and brand which makes incredible visuals for us to work off of and present to clients, and the range and quality of materials are top notch. And I love that we can happily use and support a female-led brand in this area."

ANNE MCDONALD, ANNE MCDONALD DESIGN

"What I love about Isla Porter is that the horizon feels wide open. On top of an elevated and varied collection of offerings, there are endless ways to introduce personalized details — a millwork piece, the ability to specify a custom color, and so on. It makes our team excited to dig in and play!"

LIZ HOEKZEMA, KLH CUSTOM HOMES

"It has been such a joy to work with Isla Porter. I typically work with new vendors on personal projects, so that I can experience the quality of the product and level of service first-hand. This was the case with our first project with Isla Porter. I worked with Briauna and her team on a kitchen remodel in an historic farmhouse. It was crucial that the design was sensitive to the history of the home, and the Isla Porter team helped me to get it just right! Their process is so streamlined and detailed - it made designing our kitchen a breeze. The communication and service have been top-notch, and the installation was seamless - and yes, the cabinets are fantastic. Our installer even commented on how well-made they are. Isla Porter represents a brilliant and beautiful concept, excellently done. I can't wait to work with them again!"

ALISON GIESE, ALISON GIESE INTERIORS

"Isla Porter’s aesthetic is unmistakable—tailored, historically informed, and quietly bold. But what stood out most was the heft of the cabinets: substantial, finely finished, and clearly built to last. Their design team meets you where you are being as technical or full service as you desire; they are here for you on your journey to craft your perfect kitchen."

COLIN WIENCEK, HOMEOWNER

STAY INSPIRED
AND INFORMED

metadata.title: Last Year You Said Next Year – Linear
metadata.description: It’s time to push back against the forces of stagnation. It’s time for a change.
metadata.ogTitle: Last Year You Said Next Year – Linear
metadata.ogDescription: It’s time to push back against the forces of stagnation. It’s time for a change.
h1s: Last Year You Said Next Year
h2s: It’s time to push back against the forces of stagnation. It’s time for change.
metadata.textContent:
Skip to content →
Product
Resources
Pricing
Customers
Now
Contact
Log in
Sign up
Last Year
You Said
Next Year
It’s time to push back against the forces of stagnation. It’s time for change.
Chapter I

Change is a curious thing.

Some people will tell you that change is impossible. That it should be resisted. That it goes against human nature.

But change isn’t optional. It happens all the time.

Day by day. Minute by minute. Second by second. Tirelessly replacing old with new.

In an oxymoronic sense, change is the only constant we have in life.

Change is the delta between today and tomorrow. It’s the discrepancy between who we are and who we want to be. The diff between the status quo and the future we desire.

Without change everything would remain the same. Ambitions would remain dreams and dreams would remain fantasies.

Change is inevitable, but it doesn’t happen automatically. There’s a voice of resistance that’s trying to hold it back.

Change
Change
Change
Change
Chapter II

That voice of resistance is always there.

It appears right after you wake up, commanding the muscles in your hand to hit the snooze button. It shows up late at night, trying to keep you from setting the alarm in the first place. It’s constantly in your head, continuously telling you that the status quo is good enough.

“Just do it tomorrow,” it whispers.

But tomorrow never comes.

What comes is stagnation. A futureless future that’s stuck in the past.

The voice of resistance is not just talking to you. Stagnation also happens on a collective level. It happens when change is systematically held back by the inertia of our own shared beliefs. When we become trapped in a dictatorless dystopia worshiping an imaginary “truth”.

We call this truth “the standard”, “the norm”, or “common sense”. We treat it like a law of physics that can’t be changed or broken. But the reality is that this truth is just a disguised version of the decaying status quo. It’s a made-up concept designed for a world that no longer exists. A cargo cult.

Breaking out of this prison of stagnation requires technological progress. It requires tools that allow us to communicate, collaborate and co-create.

In the world of today, most of these tools appear in the form of software, built upon a shared language we call code. They are spreadsheets, messaging clients, version control systems, text editors, and issue trackers.

These tools enable us to work together as teams, movements, states, and companies. They allow us to escape stagnation and collectively push for change.

But what happens when our tools for change become the things that hold us back?

Stagnation
Stagnation
Stagnation
Chapter III

Reflect on the current state of your work and you’ll realize that things are not as they used to be.

Where did all these meetings come from?

When did everything become so incredibly slow?

What happened to shipping things fast and frequently?

Why does your issue tracker require you to fill 37 different drop-down menus?

What you are witnessing are the forces of stagnation. Slowly pouring sand in the gears of your once so nimble team. Steadily slowing you down to the point where you are unable to move.

The stasis of your team is a direct result of the stasis of your tools. Stagnation is invisible but you can feel its presence in the form of bloated user interfaces, loading spinners, and endless UX papercuts.

With decaying tools comes a decaying spirit. Complacency slowly creeps in. Ambition gradually fades away. What’s left is a quiet acceptance of the emptiness of your once so sparkling existence. Why choose change when the voice of resistance offers you a thousand paths of least resistance?

New excuse
We’ve always done it this way…
Give in
New excuse
It’s good enough.
Fine
New excuse
We’ll do it next year!
Sure…
New excuse
What we have is good enough.
Comply
New excuse
It’s not worth the effort.
Give up
New excuse
It’s good enough.
Fine

But stagnation never stops. It doesn’t magically disappear. Life doesn’t get better by chance. It only gets better by change. To rejuvenate your team you must rejuvenate your tools. To leave the status quo behind you must change your tools for change.

Tools for
Change
Chapter IV

Change is a curious thing.

Some people will tell you change is impossible. That it should be resisted. That it goes against human nature.

But change is inevitable. It’s the only certainty in life.

Change is the island of hope in the ocean of complacency. It’s your protection against the rising sea levels of stagnation. A lighthouse that’s guiding you to a better future.

The future it points to is one where you are free from the chores and chains of your old legacy tools. A future where your team is rejuvenated and ready to create truly magical things again.

That future is not an inconceivable dream, but simply a matter of time and choice. All it needs is a final gentle push.

The island of hope is right in front of you.

You have felt its shores be

description.pageDescription:
This is a sophisticated, minimalist landing page with a strong vertical layout structure. The page is organized into distinct chapters, creating a narrative flow:

The layout follows a single-column design with generous whitespace and features:
- A stark black background transitioning to subtle gradients
- Large, pixel-perfect typography using a mix of serif and sans-serif fonts
- Systematic grid layouts showing workflow diagrams and process maps
- Chapter-based navigation with clear hierarchy (Chapters I through IV)
- Pixel art/retro-style illustrations showing connected boxes and pathways
- Strong typographic contrast between the pixelated "Change" text and clean body copy
- Bottom navigation bar with minimal icons and labels
- Subtle gradient overlays creating depth and dimension

The visual elements employ:
- Monochromatic color scheme (black, white, and grays)
- Pixel art elements suggesting digital transformation
- Geometric shapes and connecting lines showing process flows
- Minimal use of color to maintain focus on content
- Clean, crisp borders and subtle shadow effects
- Modernist grid systems for information organization
description.style: Neo-brutalist meets pixel art, with a sophisticated minimalist foundation. The design combines retro gaming aesthetics with modern corporate sensibilities.
description.audience: Technology professionals, particularly product managers, developers, and digital transformation leaders in modern tech companies.
description.pageType: Product landing page / Feature announcement page
description.layoutStyle: Experimental/Creative - Breaking conventional landing page patterns while maintaining clear information hierarchy
description.intent:
The page aims to inspire action around organizational change and digital transformation. The "Last Year You Said Next Year" headline creates urgency, while the structured chapter approach guides visitors through a transformation narrative. The primary goal appears to be driving adoption of change management tools or methodologies.

The pixel art elements and retro-gaming aesthetic create an approachable way to discuss serious business transformation topics, while the clean typography and spacing maintain professional credibility. The design successfully balances playful elements with enterprise-grade sophistication.

description.pageDescription:
The page features a striking dark theme layout with a sophisticated, tech-forward design. The main hero section dominates the viewport with "Build agents with a modern TypeScript stack" as the prominent headline. The layout employs a single-column structure with generous negative space.

Key layout elements include:
- A minimal top navigation bar with logo and essential menu items
- A centered hero section with large typography
- A distinctive visual element showing circular/orbital patterns with purple and blue gradient effects
- Bottom footer section with technology partner logos arranged in a horizontal strip

The visual hierarchy emphasizes the main value proposition through typography scale and positioning. The orbital graphic element adds movement and technological sophistication through its complex geometric patterns and gradient effects.

The color palette consists of:
- Deep charcoal/black background (#000000)
- White text for high contrast
- Accent colors in purples and blues for the orbital graphic
- Subtle gray tones for secondary elements

Interactive elements appear subtle but purposeful, with clean hover states and minimal animations. The design maintains excellent readability while projecting technical sophistication.
description.style: Modern minimalist with a tech-forward aesthetic, incorporating elements of glassmorphism in the orbital graphic. The design language is clean, sophisticated, and future-oriented.
description.audience: Technical professionals, specifically developers and engineers working with TypeScript and modern development stacks. The design choices suggest an audience that appreciates technical sophistication and clean, efficient interfaces.
description.layoutStyle: Modern and conventional with subtle experimental elements, particularly in the orbital visualization. The layout follows established patterns while incorporating distinctive visual elements that set it apart.
description.intent:
The page aims to:
- Introduce Mastra.ai's TypeScript-based agent building platform
- Position the product as modern and sophisticated
- Drive technical audience engagement
- Convert visitors into users/customers through clear value proposition
- Establish credibility through technology partnerships

The design effectively balances professionalism with technical innovation, creating an environment that would appeal to developers while maintaining enterprise-grade credibility.

description.pageDescription:
The page features a modern, airy layout with a sophisticated grid system that combines full-width sections with asymmetrical content blocks. The design employs a distinctive pastel color palette dominated by soft pinks, blues, and coral gradients that create an ethereal, contemporary atmosphere.

Layout breakdown:
- Hero section with large headline and gradient background
- Product showcase area with floating video/image cards
- Feature grid with alternating text and visual content
- Social proof section with partner logos (including Style, Fox, PHD)
- Interactive demo section showing the product interface
- Gallery strip showing content examples
- Call-to-action section at the bottom

Visual elements include:
- Floating UI components with subtle shadows
- Gradient overlays and color transitions
- Abstract hand illustrations in coral/peach tones
- Sophisticated typography combining serif and sans-serif fonts
- Ample whitespace and breathing room between sections
- Blur effects and glassmorphic elements
- Subtle animations and hover states on interactive elements
description.style: Modern minimalist with a touch of glassmorphism and organic elements. The design combines clean lines with soft gradients and natural shapes for a contemporary tech-forward aesthetic that remains approachable and creative.
description.audience: Creative professionals, content creators, and brand marketers looking for sophisticated video creation tools. The design speaks to design-conscious users who value both aesthetics and functionality.
description.layoutStyle: Modern and experimental, breaking from conventional grid structures while maintaining clear visual hierarchy and flow.
description.intent:
The page aims to showcase an AI-powered video creation tool, emphasizing its ability to help brands create engaging short-form content. The primary goal is to drive sign-ups by demonstrating the platform's sophisticated capabilities while maintaining an accessible, user-friendly appearance. The design communicates innovation and creativity while ensuring the product feels approachable and practical.

The overall design system successfully balances cutting-edge technology messaging with friendly, creative elements, making advanced AI video creation feel accessible to creative professionals.

description.pageDescription:
The page features a clean, modern layout organized in a responsive grid system. The structure includes:

Top Section:
- Minimalist header with the main heading "Discover real-world design inspiration."
- Navigation bar with subtle styling and clear hierarchy

Main Content Area:
- Grid display of mobile app screenshots/mockups arranged in a masonry-style layout
- App icons and brand logos displayed as small circular elements
- Each screenshot appears to be interactive/clickable
- Generous whitespace between elements creating clear visual separation
- Secondary heading "Find design patterns in seconds"

Visual Elements:
- Monochromatic color scheme with white background
- Subtle shadows and rounded corners on interface elements
- High contrast between text and background
- Clean, crisp mobile UI screenshots serving as the primary visual content
- Professional product photography of mobile devices
- Brand icons using original brand colors (Nike, various app logos)

Interactions:
- Hoverable/clickable screenshot cards
- Navigation menu items with likely hover states
- Filtering system indicated by UI elements
- Smooth scrolling gallery of mobile interface examples
description.style: Modern minimalist with a focus on showcasing content. Clean, professional, and content-first approach with careful attention to spacing and typography.
description.audience: UX/UI designers, product designers, mobile app developers, and design professionals looking for interface inspiration and design patterns
description.layoutStyle: Contemporary gallery-style layout with strong grid system and professional information architecture
description.intent:
- Primary: Provide a searchable repository of mobile design patterns and inspiration
- Secondary: Allow designers to explore real-world examples of mobile interfaces
- Tertiary: Create a professional resource for the design community

The site appears to be a design tool/platform for professionals to discover and analyze mobile interface designs, with a clear focus on usability and efficient browsing of examples. The layout prioritizes the content (mobile UI screenshots) while maintaining professional polish and ease of navigation.

CreditCardLockup(
    name: $name,
    cardNumber: $cardNumber,
    month: $month,
    year: $year,
    code: $cvc
)
.entryFieldStyle(.rounded)
VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 8) {
    VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 4) {
        Text("Cardholder Name")
            .font(.caption)
        HStack {
            Spacer(minLength: 16)
            if validationErrors.contains(where: \.isNameError) {
                Image(systemName: "exclamationmark.triangle")
                    .frame(width: iconSize, height: iconSize)
                    .foregroundColor(.red)
            } else {
                Image(systemName: "person")
                    .frame(width: iconSize, height: iconSize)
            }
            TextField("Jane Doe", text: $name)
                .focused($selectedField, equals: .name)
                .textContentType(.name)
                .disableAutocorrection(true)
        }
        .frame(minHeight: 54, alignment: .center)
        .background(Color(UIColor.tertiarySystemFill))
        .cornerRadius(8)
        .onTapGesture {
            selectedField = .name
        }
    }

    VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 4) {
        Text("Card Number")
            .font(.caption)
        HStack {
            Spacer(minLength: 16)
            if validationErrors.contains(where: \.isCardNumberError) {
                Image(systemName: "exclamationmark.triangle")
                    .frame(width: iconSize, height: iconSize)
                    .foregroundColor(.red)
            } else {
                Image(systemName: "creditcard")
                    .frame(width: iconSize, height: iconSize)
            }

            let text = Binding<String> {
                formattedCardNumberString(from: cardNumber)
            } set: { string in
                cardNumber = editingCardNumberString(from: string)
            }
            TextField("4000 0027 6000 0016", text: text)
                .focused($selectedField, equals: .cardNumber)
                .textContentType(.creditCardNumber)
                .autocapitalization(.none)
                .disableAutocorrection(true)
                .keyboardType(.numberPad)
                .transformEnvironment(\.font) { font in
                    font = (font ?? .body).monospacedDigit()
                }
            Spacer()
            switch issuer {
            case .americanExpress?:
                Image("American Express")
            case .dinersClub?:
                Image("Diners Club")
            case .discover?:
                Image("Discover")
            case .jcb?:
                Image("JCB")
            case .masterCard?:
                Image("Mastercard")
            case .visa?:
                Image("Visa")
            case .none:
                EmptyView()
            }
            Spacer(minLength: 16)
        }
        .frame(minHeight: 54, alignment: .center)
        .background(Color(UIColor.tertiarySystemFill))
        .corn

description.pageDescription:
The page employs a sophisticated modular grid system showcasing three main project sections. The layout features generous whitespace and follows a clear vertical rhythm. Each section is presented as a full-width container with:

Top Section: "Supercharge your mobile team" showcases an e-commerce interface with a sweatshirt product page. The design presents a clean mobile app interface with product images, color selectors, size options, and an "Add to Cart" button in a vibrant blue accent color. Product details and pricing are clearly displayed with a modern typography hierarchy.

Middle Section: "Import engineering excellence" displays a dark-themed development or configuration interface with code snippets and form elements. The section uses monospace typography and terminal-style design elements with high contrast white-on-black text.

Bottom Section: "Compose anything mobile" demonstrates a mobile interface builder or customization tool. It features annotated UI components with connection lines and technical specifications, suggesting a design system or component library interface.

The overall layout maintains consistent spacing between sections, uses a monochromatic blue accent color scheme, and implements subtle hover animations on interactive elements. Typography is clean and contemporary, primarily using system fonts and Unica77 for headings.
description.style: Modern minimalist with a technical edge, combining clean interface design with development-focused elements. The aesthetic bridges professional software design with developer tooling.
description.audience: Mobile development teams, software engineers, product designers, and technical product managers working on mobile applications.

description.intent:
The page aims to demonstrate the capabilities of a mobile development platform or tool suite, encouraging technical teams to adopt the solution for their mobile app development needs. The primary call-to-action appears to be getting teams to try or implement the platform in their workflow.

The design effectively balances technical complexity with accessible presentation, making sophisticated development concepts visually approachable while maintaining professional credibility.

description.pageDescription:
This appears to be an extremely minimalist page with a stark, grid-based layout. The page consists entirely of thin horizontal lines creating a notebook or ledger-like grid pattern against a plain white/off-white background. The lines are spaced evenly, creating consistent rectangular cells across the page. There's no visible content, navigation, imagery, or interactive elements - just the geometric pattern of lines forming a grid structure.

The grid appears to be constructed with:
- Thin, light gray horizontal lines
- Equal spacing between lines
- Full-width layout
- No vertical lines except possibly at margins
- No content or elements overlaid on the grid

The overall effect is reminiscent of graph paper, accounting ledgers, or traditional Japanese grid paper (Genkō yōshi).
description.style: Ultra-minimalist, borderline brutalist in its stripped-down aesthetic. The design embraces negative space and geometric simplicity to an extreme degree.
description.audience:
Given the stark minimalism, this could be targeting:
- Design professionals
- Artists
- Architects
- People interested in minimal aesthetics
- Creative studios
description.pageType:
Landing page/homepage, though in its current state it appears to be either:
- A placeholder
- An intentionally minimal artistic statement
- Part of an interactive experience that hasn't loaded
description.layoutStyle: Experimental/artistic - the complete absence of conventional website elements makes this an unconventional approach that challenges traditional web design patterns.
description.intent:
The page appears to be either:
- Making an artistic statement through extreme minimalism
- Serving as a canvas for future content
- Creating intrigue through deliberate emptiness
- Functioning as part of a larger interactive experience

The stark simplicity suggests intentionality in the design choice, likely aiming to create curiosity or establish a unique brand identity through radical minimalism.

Font detection indicates GrosaMono and sans fonts are present, though no visible text is shown in the screenshot, suggesting there may be hidden or dynamic content elements not visible in this static view.

description.pageDescription:
The page features a sophisticated single-column layout that opens with a striking hero section. The main focal point is a lifestyle product shot showing someone wearing light-colored headphones while working at a laptop, dressed in a warm orange/rust-colored sweater. The scene is thoughtfully composed with natural lighting from a window and includes desk accessories like a pencil holder and desk lamp, creating a cozy work-from-home atmosphere.

Below the hero, the page showcases three main product offerings:
- Tadpole ("The tiny webcam with a clip")
- Opal C1 ("The first professional webcam")
- Composer ("The magic behind the camera")

Each product is presented with a minimal product image against a clean background, accompanied by a "Now $99" or similar CTA button.

The navigation is clean and minimal, with main sections for Products, Company, and Resources. The Opal logo is prominent in the header, and the design maintains generous whitespace throughout.

The color palette is understated and professional:
- Black and white as primary colors
- Yellow accent for CTAs
- Subtle grays for secondary elements
- Warm environmental colors in photography

The footer contains newsletter signup, company information, and social links, organized in a clear hierarchical structure.
description.style: Modern minimalist with a premium, sophisticated edge. The design embraces clean lines, ample whitespace, and high-quality photography while maintaining a professional and refined aesthetic.
description.audience: Creative professionals, remote workers, and tech-savvy individuals who value quality video conferencing equipment and are willing to invest in premium hardware for their home office setup.
description.layoutStyle: Contemporary and conventional, following established e-commerce best practices while maintaining a premium feel. The layout prioritizes product storytelling through high-quality imagery and minimal, impactful copy.
description.intent:
The page aims to:
- Establish Opal as a premium webcam brand
- Showcase their product line, particularly highlighting "the first webcam designed for laptops"
- Drive sales through clear product presentation and pricing
- Build brand credibility through professional design and photography
- Capture leads through newsletter signup

The design successfully combines product marketing with lifestyle aspiration, positioning Opal's webcams as essential tools for the modern remote worker who values quality and aesthetics.

description.pageDescription:
The page features a striking minimalist design dominated by a hero section with two main elements: the "Focale" logotype/wordmark displayed in white against a dark background, and a distinctive oval/pill-shaped element with a blue gradient fill. The layout employs significant negative space and follows a clean, asymmetrical composition. Below the hero section, there's a subtle decorative element of green, leaf-like shapes creating an organic pattern that contrasts with the geometric elements above.

The layout follows a single-column structure with careful attention to whitespace and breathing room between elements. The typography is set in Lausanne-Regular, a modern sans-serif that reinforces the contemporary feel. The overall spatial organization prioritizes simplicity and impact, with each element given ample room to create visual tension and interest.

The color palette is carefully curated:
- Deep navy/black for the background
- Vibrant royal blue for the oval element
- White for typography
- Various shades of green for the decorative foliage elements

The interaction design appears minimal and purposeful, with the oval element likely serving as a focal point for user interaction.
description.style: Ultra-minimalist with a contemporary edge, combining geometric modernism with organic elements. The design language draws from Swiss/International Style with a current digital-first interpretation.
description.audience: Creative professionals, design agencies, and sophisticated brand-conscious clients. The refined aesthetic suggests a focus on high-end, design-savvy customers who appreciate minimalism and contemporary design.
description.pageType: Homepage/Landing page, likely serving as an introduction to a creative studio or design-focused business.
description.layoutStyle: Experimental and artistic, breaking from conventional website layouts to create a more sculptural, gallery-like presentation that prioritizes visual impact over traditional navigation patterns.
description.intent:
The page appears designed to make a strong first impression and establish brand positioning through sophisticated design choices. It likely serves as a gateway to a portfolio or creative services offering, aiming to communicate design expertise through its execution rather than explicit messaging. The minimal approach suggests confidence and exclusivity.

The site effectively balances artistic expression with functional design, creating an memorable entry point that sets high expectations for the content to follow.

What is Popcorn?

Popcorn is a simple, all-in-one global phone plan that just works. Enjoy unlimited talk, text, and data worldwide for one price. No hidden fees, roaming charges or hassle.

What countries are you available in?

We’re live in 180+ countries. The only places we haven’t seen users are a few remote islands or unstable regions like North Korea.

Can I keep my current phone number?

Absolutely, we support US numbers and you can transfer yours directly in the app. Or grab a fresh one if you’d like.

If your phone supports eSIM and is from the last 5 years, you’re good to go.

$69/mo including taxes. That’s it.

description.pageDescription:
The page follows a clean, vertical scrolling layout with well-defined sections and strong visual hierarchy. The design uses a combination of centered content blocks and full-width sections.

Key sections from top to bottom:
1. Hero section with bold typography "One global plan. No hassle." and two floating phone mockups with app interface screenshots
2. Feature highlights showing three key benefits with icons
3. Pricing section titled "A US number, with peace of mind" featuring a $69 monthly fee
4. Three-column benefits section with mint green cards showing "Simple", "Fast & Easy", and "Export Friendly" features
5. Coverage section with logos of supported carriers on a dotted background
6. Testimonials section with quote cards
7. FAQ section with expandable questions
8. Bottom CTA section with phone mockup and "Get connected in 72 seconds" offer

Visual Elements:
- Color palette: Predominantly white background with mint green accents, soft pink highlights
- Typography: Clean combination of Messina Sans for body text and Untitled Serif for headings
- Generous whitespace throughout
- Subtle shadows and gradients on phone mockups
- Rounded corners on cards and buttons
- Dotted pattern backgrounds for visual texture
- Professional app interface mockups showing the product
description.style: Modern minimalist with a premium feel, using contemporary design trends like floating devices and subtle gradients
description.audience: Digital nomads, international professionals, and business travelers who need global connectivity solutions
description.layoutStyle: Contemporary single-page scroll with modular sections following current SaaS website conventions
description.intent:
The page aims to convert visitors into subscribers for their global phone plan service. The primary CTA encourages users to sign up for the $69/month plan, emphasizing the simplicity and hassle-free nature of the service. The design builds trust through transparent pricing, clear feature explanation, and social proof elements.

The combination of clean typography, ample whitespace, and professional imagery creates a sense of reliability and sophistication that appeals to business professionals seeking a premium communication solution.

The fonts and design elements work together to convey a modern, trustworthy service that simplifies global communication needs.

The page features a clean, minimalist layout dominated by a light blue gradient background that creates a serene, aqueous atmosphere. At the top right, there's a newsletter signup form with a simple input field on a semi-transparent white background. The main content area is centered and features the brand name "Postevand" in large, bold typography using what appears to be Nimbus Sans D OT. The layout employs significant whitespace and follows a single-column structure with careful attention to vertical rhythm and spacing.

The navigation appears minimal, with only essential elements visible. The top-right corner contains utility items including what appears to be a menu icon. The overall composition is purposefully sparse, creating a sense of calm and clarity. There's a subtle interplay between the background gradient and the foreground elements, with careful attention to contrast and readability.

description.pageDescription:
The page features a modern, gradient-rich layout showcasing a crypto wallet application. The design follows a vertical scrolling structure with multiple distinct sections:

- Header: Features a clean, minimal navigation with the Rainbow logo and key actions
- Hero Section: Large, bold typography announcing "Experience" with supporting copy about wallet functionality
- Product Showcase: Multiple floating UI cards demonstrating the wallet interface with gradient backgrounds and subtle shadows
- Keyboard Section: Displays an aesthetic mechanical keyboard visualization highlighting shortcut functionality
- Wallet Management Section: Shows circular interface elements arranged in a spiral pattern demonstrating cross-chain compatibility
- Features Grid: Clean, organized presentation of key features with accompanying icons and explanatory text

The color palette is vibrant and playful, heavily featuring:
- Pastel gradients (pink to blue)
- Bold orange accents
- Dark mode UI elements
- White space for breathing room
- Rainbow-inspired multi-color elements

Interactive elements include:
- Floating cards with hover animations
- Subtle parallax scrolling effects
- Animated UI demonstrations
- Clear CTAs with hover states
description.style: Modern and playful with a strong emphasis on glassmorphism and gradient aesthetics. The design combines contemporary crypto/Web3 visual language with an approachable, consumer-friendly feel.
description.audience: Crypto-curious mainstream users and Web3 enthusiasts who want a more user-friendly wallet experience. The design choices suggest targeting both crypto newcomers and experienced users looking for a more refined interface.
description.layoutStyle: Modern and experimental, breaking from traditional grid layouts in favor of organic spacing and floating elements. The design employs contemporary trends like asymmetric layouts and overlapping elements.
description.intent:
The page aims to:
1. Showcase the Rainbow wallet as a user-friendly crypto management tool
2. Drive downloads and adoption
3. Demonstrate the product's ease of use and security features
4. Position the brand as a premium yet accessible crypto wallet solution

The design successfully balances sophisticated functionality with approachable aesthetics, making complex crypto operations feel more accessible to mainstream users.

description.pageDescription:
This is a dark-themed developer tool landing page with a sophisticated, tech-focused design. The layout follows a single-column structure with generous vertical spacing between sections.

Layout breakdown:
- Hero section features "Email for developers" headline with supporting text and partner logos (Vercel, Miex, Discord, etc.)
- Code snippet section showing integration examples with syntax highlighting in purple/green
- "First-class developer experience" section with code examples and terminal-style interfaces
- Editor demonstration section with video/interactive preview
- Features section with metrics and capability highlights
- React development section showing code samples
- Final CTA section focused on deliverability

The design employs:
- Dark gray/black backgrounds (#000000) with white text
- Accent colors: purple (#7857FF) and mint green (#3FE1B0)
- Monospace fonts for code blocks
- Terminal-style interfaces with rounded corners
- Subtle gradient overlays and glow effects
- Clean typography with clear hierarchy
- Interactive code editors and live preview sections
- Consistent padding and whitespace
- Subtle hover animations on interactive elements
- Logo grid showing integration partners
- Code syntax highlighting in theme-appropriate colors
description.style: Modern minimalist with a developer-focused aesthetic. Combines dark mode with subtle glassmorphic elements and terminal-inspired interfaces.
description.audience: Software developers and technical teams looking for email infrastructure solutions. The technical nature of the content and prominence of code examples clearly targets a technical audience.
description.layoutStyle: Modern and conventional for developer tools, following established patterns from companies like Vercel and Stripe. Single-column narrative flow with alternating text/code sections.
description.intent:
Primary goal is to convince developers to integrate this email service into their applications. The page aims to:
1. Demonstrate technical capability and ease of integration
2. Show developer-friendly features
3. Build credibility through partner logos
4. Drive sign-ups through prominent CTAs
5. Emphasize developer experience and simplicity

The design effectively balances technical depth with visual sophistication, making complex functionality appear accessible and professional.

In our universe, there's no room for political maneuvering or rigid mindsets. We value collaboration, creativity, and a positive work environment over office politics and manipulative tactics.

Motion

description.pageDescription:
The page follows a modern, dynamic layout with an unconventional typographic treatment. Here's a detailed breakdown:

Layout Structure:
- Full-width design with asymmetrical grid
- Generous whitespace and breathing room between sections
- Overlapping elements create depth and visual interest
- Mixed-width columns that adapt to content

Sections:
1. Header: Minimal navigation with logo and menu items
2. Hero section: Features distorted typography "Gett ready for a design accelleratttion"
3. Portfolio grid: Showcases work samples in a masonry-style layout
4. Interactive elements scattered throughout with hover states
5. Text sections with varying typography sizes and weights

Visual Elements:
- Black and white base palette with selective color accents
- Experimental typography with intentional distortion effects
- Custom cursor treatment
- Subtle animations and transitions
- Mix of UI screenshots and photography
- Strong typographic hierarchy with dramatic size contrasts

Interactions:
- Hover effects on portfolio items
- Smooth scrolling animations
- Interactive cursor following movement
- Dynamic menu transitions
- Clickable case study previews
description.style: Contemporary experimental design with strong brutalist influences. Combines clean minimalism with intentionally distorted elements for a rebellious, artistic approach.
description.audience: Creative professionals, design agencies, and forward-thinking brands looking for innovative design solutions. Appeals to those who appreciate experimental design and aren't afraid to break conventional rules.
description.pageType: Homepage/Portfolio hybrid serving as both introduction and work showcase
description.layoutStyle: Highly experimental and creative, breaking traditional grid systems while maintaining usability. Uses unconventional typography and spacing to create visual interest.
description.intent:
- Establish the studio as a bold, innovative design practice
- Showcase their unique approach through the website's own design
- Attract clients who value experimental, cutting-edge design
- Drive potential clients to explore their work and make contact

The site effectively communicates a design studio that pushes boundaries while maintaining professional credibility through well-executed experimental elements.

metadata.ogDescription: Simplify fundraises and clean up your cap table
h2s: Used by companies at every stage
Top law firms & companies trust us to consolidate stakeholders
metadata.textContent:
rollups
Roll Up Vehicles
Consolidation
SAFEs
Resources
SIGN IN
GET STARTED

Announcing Rollups: bringing companies Roll Up Vehicles® (RUVs) and more.

READ ANNOUNCEMENT
×
Take control of your equity

Rollups makes it easy for companies to consolidate many stakeholders into one vehicle

GET STARTED

Rollups is trusted by over 50,000 investors and companies

ROLLUPS SOLUTIONS

Two ways companies use Rollups
Raise

Raise capital with Roll Up Vehicles® (RUVs) from many investors in one entity — and one line on your cap table. Powered by AngelList.

LEARN MORE
Consolidate

Move existing stakeholders into a Consolidation Vehicle to consolidate their ownership into one entity — and one line on the cap table.

LEARN MORE

01

Raise with Roll Up Vehicles®

02

Consolidate legacy stakeholders

03

Streamline acquisitions

Rollups in action

Three common situations in which companies use Rollups. Don't see your use case? There are many more. Contact us.

Pretty sure that RUVs are the single best fintech product ever launched. I bet NPS (for both the investors and the founders) is off the charts.

Auren Hoffman, @auren

SHOW TESTIMONIAL

RAISE

Raise with Roll Up Vehicles®

Raise capital from many investors in a single entity — and just one line on your cap table.

Your startup wants to include 45 friends, advisors, and angels in your round.

Rather than manage 45 separate investments, you open an RUV and invite them to invest.

Rollups provides a smooth online investor experience for KYC/AML, accreditation, and banking, all powered by AngelList.

Track round progress, close in a few clicks, sign once, receive one wire, and just one line to your cap table.

LEARN MORE

Motif signed over 30 SAFEs to raise initial funds. We feared chasing down all of those investor signatures in future financings. But our investors were happy to execute the Rollup, saving Motif ~$20,000 and ~2 weeks time.

Marie Schneegans, Co-Founder, Markprompt

SHOW TESTIMONIAL

CONSOLIDATE

Consolidate legacy stakeholders

Regain control of your cap table by transferring early angels, SAFE holders, and small check investors into a single vehicle.

Your startup previously raised capital from 120 smaller investors.

You’re now raising a Series B, and the feedback from a potential lead is that your cap table is too messy.

You create a Consolidation Vehicle to compile the smaller shareholders into one entity, resulting in 74 fewer lines on your cap table.

Your Series B now requires 74 fewer signatures to close, and you're able to raise without any issues or delays.

LEARN MORE

AngelList’s consolidation vehicle was instrumental in streamlining Wand’s acquisition of Accern by combining dozens of our stakeholders into a single SPV, preserving a clean cap table for Wand.

Kumesh Aroomoogan, CEO at Accern, a Wand AI Company

SHOW TESTIMONIAL

CONSOLIDATE

Streamline acquisitions

Consolidate the acquisition target’s stakeholders into one entity before adding them to your cap table and inflating your costs.

Your startup is entertaining an acquisition offer: part cash, part equity in their company.

Issuing equity to the 120 stakeholders on your cap table creates complexity and cost for the acquirer.

So, you open a Consolidation Vehicle to consolidate all 120 stakeholders into one entity.

Now, the acquirer issues the equity to the CV once, adding just one line to their cap table, and you're able to close the deal on time.

LEARN MORE
Used by companies at every stage
PRE-SEED
Raise initial capital
from friends & family
SEED
Make room for small
investors alongside VCs
SERIES A
Conduct fast second
closes for follow-on investors
SERIES C
Raise RUVs to
fund employee liquidity
SERIES D+
Streamline
secondaries

Rollups scales with you.

Startups have used Roll Up Vehicles® (RUVs) to raise anywhere from $50k to $100M+.

Growth-stage companies leverage Consolidation Vehicles (CVs) to simplify complex cap tables, and avoid friction with secondaries, liquidity events, or acquisitions.

GET STARTED
Top law firms & companies trust us to consolidate stakeholders
Everything You Need to Know
What’s the difference between Rollups and Roll Up Vehicles® (RUVs)?
Rollups is the name of our company. Our products “rollup” companies’ stakeholders when raising capital and when consolidating existing stakeholders. When raising capital, companies use Roll Up Vehicles® (RUVs). When consolidating existing investors, it’s called a Consolidation Vehicle.
What is Rollups.com's relationship with AngelList?
Rollups is part of AngelList’s ecosystem of brands. AngelList.com is for VCs and investors. Rollups serves companies. Our products for fundraising and consolidating stakeholders benefit from AngelList’s core infrastructure. Your investors and stakeholders likely already have AngelList accounts, and they can use them with Rollups without any additional effort.
Can I use Rollups with my existing cap table software?
Yes. All of Rollups’ products work with any cap table solution.
I have qu

description.pageDescription:
The page employs a sophisticated single-column layout with a dark green (#054C3C) background that creates a premium, financial-sector feel. The layout follows a vertical narrative structure with clearly defined sections:

- Header section with "Take control of your equity" as the main headline
- "Two ways companies use Rollups" section with illustrative diagrams
- "Rollups in action" featuring step-by-step process illustrations
- "Securely empowers at every stage" section
- Social proof section with company logos
- FAQ/Information section titled "Everything You Need to Know"

The visual hierarchy is carefully constructed with consistent typography using SuisseIntl for body text and AbcFavoritMono for technical elements. White and light green text (#E5E5E5) contrasts effectively against the deep green background. The design makes extensive use of geometric shapes, subtle gradients, and clean lines to create visual interest.

Interactive elements include:
- Subtle hover states on buttons
- Expandable FAQ sections
- Navigation menu items with clear hierarchy
- CTA buttons with light backgrounds for contrast

The spacing is generous and deliberate, using a consistent grid system that allows content to breathe while maintaining visual connection between related elements.
description.style: Modern corporate minimalism with a premium fintech aesthetic. The design combines contemporary clean lines with sophisticated financial sector credibility markers.
description.audience: Primary audience appears to be corporate financial decision-makers, equity managers, and company executives who need to manage equity-related processes. Secondary audience includes legal professionals and stakeholders in equity management.
description.pageType: Product landing page focused on explaining the platform's value proposition and building trust through demonstration of functionality.
description.layoutStyle: Contemporary single-column narrative layout that follows modern SaaS landing page conventions while maintaining a unique, premium feel through color choices and typography.
description.intent:
The page aims to:
1. Explain the complex concept of equity management in an accessible way
2. Build credibility through enterprise client logos and detailed product demonstrations
3. Convert visitors into leads by demonstrating both expertise and ease of use
4. Encourage visitors to engage with the platform through clear calls-to-action

The design successfully balances professional credibility with modern SaaS accessibility, using sophisticated visual language to communicate trustworthiness in handling sensitive financial matters.

metadata.title: Side Stage Ventures
metadata.description: Side Stage Ventures is a founder-led seed fund backing outlier founders in Australia.  We write the first  cheque into the best early stage companies. Then we lean in to help the founders build & scale.
metadata.ogDescription: Side Stage Ventures is a founder-led seed fund backing outlier founders in Australia.  We write the first  cheque into the best early stage companies. Then we lean in to help the founders build & scale.
h1s: WE’RE BUILDING THE SEED FUND WE WISH WE HAD WHEN WE WERE STARTING OUT.
metadata.textContent:

WE’RE BUILDING THE SEED FUND WE WISH WE HAD WHEN WE WERE STARTING OUT.
A FOUNDER-LED SEED FUND

WE’VE EXPERIENCED SUCCESS AND WE’VE EXPERIENCED FAILURE BUT MOST IMPORTANTLY… WE DEEPLY UNDERSTAND THE STRUGGLE OF BUILDING A BUSINESS FROM THE GROUND UP.

WE WRITE THE FIRST CHEQUE INTO THE BEST EARLY STAGE COMPANIES. THEN WE LEAN IN TO HELP THE FOUNDERS BUILD & SCALE.

WE BACK PEOPLE.
WE’RE COMFORTABLE INVESTING ACROSS ALL SECTORS AND BUSINESS MODELS.

AND OUR GOAL IS SIMPLE:
TO BE THE MOST VALUED EARLY-STAGE PARTNER FOR THE TOP 1% OF FOUNDERS IN AUSTRALIA.

(
HOW
WE INVEST
)

We typically invest up to $500k in pre-seed or seed rounds.

We are super collaborative and enjoy investing alongside other great investors both in Australia and around the world.

We are sector agnostic but looking for the best surfers riding the biggest waves — outlier founders, building in big markets.

We are early stage specialists — we focus on solving the challenges of 0–1.

It’s never too early. We’re comfortable backing companies very early, often pre-revenue.

(
WHY
FOUNDERS WORK WITH US
)
(
PORTFOLIO
PORTFOLIO
)
(
TEAM
TEAM
)
(
ETHOS
ETHOS
)

We are founders and we behave like founders — with passion, enthusiasm and belief.

We are flexible on valuation and ownership. We think what’s best for the founder is best for us.

We offer active and collaborative support focussed on the earliest stages of building a business — such as hiring, fundraising and getting to product market fit.

We have a high Helpfulness to Cheque Size ratio (H:CS)

We’re building a unique community of founders who want to contribute.

We are an global team with a global network.

We feel fortunate to be here and absolutely LOVE what we do.

(
WHAT
WE LOOK FOR
)
OUTLIER FOUNDERS

First and foremost, we back people. We look for outlier founders who have proven hustle, a compelling, global vision, the hunger and drive to build a billion dollar business and the capability to sell their vision to employees, partners and future investors.

DIFFERENTIATED PRODUCT

We look for products that are solving real and meaningful problems, with passionate first customers and that have the potential to be orders of magnitude better than their nearest competitor. This could be demonstrated through sticky initial customers, accessing an underserved market or through a compelling and non-consensus product insight.

BIG MARKETS

Our focus is sector agnostic but with a bias towards big markets that have either billions of potential customers or billions of dollars in accessible revenue capacity.

description.pageDescription:
The page employs a stark, minimalist design with a dramatic dark background. The layout follows a single-column structure with carefully considered typography and spacing. Key sections include:

- Header: Features "SIDE STAGE VENTURES" in a monospaced font, positioned top-left
- Hero Section: Large statement text in white against the dark background
- Main Content: Text blocks are arranged vertically with significant negative space between sections
- Portfolio Grid: Bottom section showing a horizontal scroll of project thumbnails/logos in monochrome
- Gradient Accents: Subtle teal/purple gradient elements provide visual interest without overwhelming

The typography is predominantly monospaced, creating a technical, code-like aesthetic. Text blocks are left-aligned with generous whitespace, creating a strong vertical rhythm. The contrast between the dark background and white text creates high legibility and dramatic impact.

Interactive elements appear subtle and refined, with minimal hover states and understated transitions. The portfolio section features a horizontal scrolling gallery with consistent thumbnail sizing.
description.style: Modern Minimalist with strong Brutalist influences. The design embraces negative space, monospaced typography, and high contrast, creating a sophisticated tech-forward aesthetic that feels both contemporary and slightly experimental.
description.audience: Early-stage startup founders, tech entrepreneurs, and venture capital professionals. The design speaks to a tech-savvy audience that appreciates refined minimalism and understated sophistication.
description.pageType: Homepage/Landing Page with integrated portfolio elements
description.layoutStyle: Experimental/Modern - The layout breaks from conventional patterns with its dramatic use of whitespace and typography-focused design. While unconventional, it remains highly readable and purposeful.
description.intent:
The page aims to establish Side Stage Ventures as a modern, founder-friendly venture capital firm. The messaging and design work together to convey approachability while maintaining professional credibility. The primary goal appears to be brand positioning and encouraging potential founders to learn more about their investment thesis and portfolio.

The design successfully balances being distinctive and experimental while remaining functional and professional - appropriate for a forward-thinking VC firm targeting early-stage founders.

description.pageDescription:
The website employs a bold, experimental layout that combines stark typography with dramatic fire/smoke imagery and minimalist white space. The page is structured in a dynamic grid system with asymmetrical sections that create visual tension and interest.

Key sections include:
- Hero area featuring large numerical typography ("23/25") overlaid on vibrant orange-red flame imagery
- Navigation using clean sans-serif typography, minimally positioned
- Portfolio preview sections showing architectural-style photography of what appears to be physical models or displays
- Text sections using a strong typographic hierarchy with clean, modernist fonts
- "NOPE/YES" contrast statements in bold typography
- Footer section with location identifier "LONDON"

The color palette is striking and limited:
- Deep orange and red tones from flame imagery
- Pure black and white creating maximum contrast
- Subtle grays for secondary information

Visual elements utilize generous negative space, sharp typography, and a combination of full-width imagery with precise geometric layouts. The design demonstrates careful attention to typographic scale and positioning.
description.style: Contemporary Brutalist with elements of Swiss/International Style typography. The aesthetic is bold, unapologetic, and pushes conventional boundaries while maintaining strong design principles.
description.audience: Creative professionals, design-forward brands, and businesses seeking distinctive brand identities. The sophisticated design approach suggests targeting clients who value experimental, high-end design.
description.layoutStyle: Highly experimental and creative, breaking conventional grid structures while maintaining strong visual hierarchy and purposeful composition.
description.intent:
- Establish immediate design credibility through bold aesthetic choices
- Showcase the studio's capability for distinctive, boundary-pushing design
- Attract design-conscious clients seeking unique brand expressions
- Position the studio as a high-end, selective creative partner
- Drive inquiries from ambitious brands seeking website and brand design services

The design successfully communicates a confident, design-forward approach that sets high expectations for the studio's work while filtering for clients who appreciate this bold aesthetic direction.

description.pageDescription:
The page follows a modern, dark-themed vertical layout with multiple distinct sections. The hero section features a dramatic headline "The all-in-one platform for 3D and design" with floating 3D geometric shapes in purple, blue, green, and orange that create depth and movement. Below are two rows of client/partner logos in muted white, including major brands like Google, Microsoft, and Forbes, establishing credibility.

The layout employs a sophisticated grid system with asymmetrical content blocks that maintain visual harmony. Key sections include:
- Hero with floating 3D elements and headline
- Social proof/logos section
- Portfolio/showcase grid displaying 3D design examples
- Feature blocks with yellow accent elements
- Product benefit sections with dark cards and minimal copy

The visual hierarchy emphasizes the 3D capabilities through interactive elements and sophisticated animations. The color palette centers on rich blacks (#000000) and dark grays with strategic pops of color, particularly a vibrant yellow (#FFB800) used for CTAs and accent elements.

Navigation is minimal and clean, focusing attention on the main content. Interactive elements include subtle hover states, smooth transitions, and apparent 3D manipulation capabilities demonstrated in the showcase section.
description.style: Ultra-modern with a premium feel, combining dark mode aesthetics with 3D design elements. The style bridges sophisticated minimalism with future-forward 3D capabilities, creating an immersive, high-end tech product experience.
description.audience:
Primary: 3D designers, digital artists, and creative professionals
Secondary: Design teams, creative agencies, and tech companies requiring 3D design capabilities
description.layoutStyle: Contemporary and experimental, pushing beyond conventional layouts while maintaining clear hierarchy and usability. The integration of 3D elements and asymmetrical grids creates visual interest without sacrificing functionality.
description.intent:
The page aims to position Spline as the leading all-in-one 3D design platform, encouraging users to try or adopt the tool. The primary goal appears to be driving product adoption among professional creators while showcasing the platform's capabilities through interactive demonstrations and high-quality examples.

The design effectively communicates sophisticated technology while maintaining accessibility for creative professionals who may be new to 3D design tools.

description.pageDescription:
The page follows a sophisticated, editorial-style layout with clear visual hierarchy. The structure includes:

Top Section:
- Large "NEW WORK" typography dominating the header
- Clean, minimal navigation and cookie notice
- Brief agency description in refined typography

Portfolio Grid:
- Horizontal scrolling gallery of project thumbnails
- Mix of material textures (brass, concrete, paper) in imagery
- High-contrast photography with careful attention to materiality
- Elegant spacing between items creating visual breathing room

Client Logo Section:
- Monochrome logo grid displaying brand partnerships
- Subtle, desaturated presentation of logos
- Even spacing and consistent sizing

Bottom Section:
- "LASTING FIRST IMPRESSIONS" large typography
- Artistic illustration/graphic element
- "CAN WE HELP YOU?" call-to-action
- Minimal footer elements

The color palette is restrained, featuring:
- Soft mint/sage background
- Deep navy/purple text
- White space
- Muted metallics in imagery
description.style: Contemporary Nordic minimalism with high-end editorial influences. The design balances modernist principles with warm, tactile elements and sophisticated typography. There's a clear influence from print design and high-end fashion branding.
description.audience: Design-conscious clients, luxury brands, and sophisticated businesses seeking premium brand and digital experiences. Appeals to creative directors and brand managers who value refined aesthetics.
description.layoutStyle: Modern editorial with experimental typography treatment. The layout breaks conventional patterns while maintaining clear hierarchy and usability.
description.intent:
To position Spring/Summer as a premium design agency through sophisticated visual presentation. The page aims to:
- Showcase high-caliber work
- Demonstrate design expertise
- Attract design-conscious clients
- Convey exclusivity and craftsmanship
- Generate inquiries from potential clients

The design effectively communicates a high-end positioning through masterful typography, careful space utilization, and refined material sensibilities.

description.pageDescription:
The page follows a dark-themed, single-column layout with distinct sections flowing vertically. The hero section features bold typography with "Your brand or website delivered at lightspeed" as the main headline, using Neue Montreal font. Below this is a showcase grid of project examples, including a prominent "Bjork" typography piece.

The page structure includes:
- Hero section with large typography
- Portfolio grid showing creative work samples
- Team/collaboration section with circular profile avatars arranged in a grid
- Service offerings split into "Brand Sprints" and "Website Sprints" sections, each with floating 3D mockups against the dark background
- "Stellar work" portfolio gallery section
- "Pressure makes diamonds" section with geometric crystal illustrations
- "How it works" process section with step-by-step visuals

The color palette centers on:
- Deep black background (#000000)
- Purple accent colors
- White text
- Strategic use of gradient effects

Visual elements include floating 3D mockups, geometric shapes, subtle purple gradients, and generous whitespace creating a premium feel. Interactions appear smooth with hover states on buttons and gallery items.
description.style: Modern minimalist with a premium tech aesthetic. Combines dark mode with sophisticated 3D elements and subtle purple accents for a high-end, future-forward feel.
description.audience: Creative professionals and brands seeking high-end design services. Appeals to design-conscious clients who value premium branding and digital experiences.
description.layoutStyle: Modern and experimental, using unconventional spacing and 3D elements while maintaining clear hierarchy and readability.
description.intent:
The page aims to showcase the agency's design capabilities and convert visitors into potential clients for their brand and website sprint services. It emphasizes speed of delivery ("lightspeed") and quality of work through visual examples and social proof from team credentials.

The design effectively communicates a premium service offering through sophisticated visuals while maintaining clarity in the service proposition and call-to-action paths.

description.pageDescription:
The page employs a clean, minimalist grid layout with significant whitespace. The structure follows a vertical flow with the following sections:

1. Header: Simple text statement "We partner with visionary founders and teams to build game-changing products."

2. Portfolio Grid: Features 6 case study projects displayed in a masonry-style layout with varying image sizes:
- Large product detail shot of what appears to be a card/device interface
- Abstract blue and white geometric visualization
- Architectural/technical blueprint-style imagery
- Mobile device interaction showing a pink interface
- Abstract 3D rendered elements in purple and gray
- Botanical photograph of white flowers against a light purple background

Each project tile includes minimal text labeling and subtle hover interactions. Images are presented in a mix of color and monochromatic styles, creating visual rhythm and contrast.

3. Footer: Centers around the statement "We are a full-service venture studio that founders trust to create products that become verbs" with a subtle CTA button below.

The overall spacing is generous, with ample padding between elements. The color palette is restrained - predominantly grays and whites with selective use of color in project imagery.
description.style: Modern minimalist with a high-end, refined aesthetic. Combines clean typography, abundant whitespace, and sophisticated imagery that bridges technical and artistic sensibilities.
description.audience: Tech founders, startup entrepreneurs, and product innovators seeking sophisticated design partnership. Appeals to those who value both technical expertise and creative excellence.
description.layoutStyle: Modern and experimental, breaking from conventional portfolio grids through varying image sizes and asymmetrical composition while maintaining clear hierarchy and navigation.
description.intent:
To showcase the studio's capability in creating innovative digital products through sophisticated case studies. The page aims to position the studio as a premium partner for ambitious founders, emphasizing both technical prowess and creative excellence. The desired action is for potential clients to explore individual case studies and ultimately initiate contact.

The design effectively balances professional credibility with creative confidence through its restrained yet impactful presentation of work.

metadata.title: A new cooperative way to create content + get paid - SuperHi Plus
metadata.description: Kickstart your content with a brand-new model of co-ownership and creator-led tools. Join our 12-week accelerator, and receive funding and support.
metadata.ogTitle: A new cooperative way to create content + get paid
metadata.ogDescription: Kickstart your content with a brand-new model of co-ownership and creator-led tools. Join our 12-week accelerator, and receive funding and support.
h2s: A new cooperative way to create content + get paid
A new cooperative way to create content and get paid
The tools you need to succeed
metadata.textContent:
…
SuperHi Plus
A new cooperative way to create content + get paid
A new cooperative way to create content and get paid
…

SuperHi Plus is a brand new model of co-ownership, with funding, support and creator-led tools, to help you kickstart your content and get paid.

Funding up to
$12,000

Program
12 weeks

Creator-led
Tools

For creators, by creators

Currently, creators face an all-or-nothing decision. Either do everything by yourself with all the risks and no guarantee of success, or by joining a platform and play to their rules.

SuperHi Plus is a brand new model of co-ownership for creators combining the best of existing content creation models, while reducing the downsides.

1
Funding + revenue
Funding and revenue

Receive between $6,000 and $12,000 funding to kickstart your content without risk or repayment, keep 80-90% ownership and start making money.

2
Creator-focused support

With our 12-week program you will never walk alone. We support you and nine other creators with tailored guidance, resources, and collaborative sessions.

3
Creator-led tools

We’ve developed flexible, open tools that don’t lock into a single platform, for you to succeed.

Who we’re looking for

Do you have ideas for content? We want to hear about it! Until now, SuperHi has specifically featured creative education courses, but your content doesn’t need to be educational or course-based. We are opening up content formats and encourage anything experimental. You do you, as long as it roughly fits with being...

Creative-focused

The SuperHi audience is spread across the globe and comprises people who work, or want to work, in creative fields. The thing that unites them is a love of making things.

Web-based

We love the internet. We’re focusing on web-based formats, so video, audio, text, interactive websites, or something brand new — anything a web browser can deliver.

Asynchronously delivered

No live sessions or Zoom classes (for now), so we can help you iterate and improve your content, plus widen its accessibility.

We’re looking for the weird and the wonderful — no topic or format would be considered too experimental.

Beyond current models

With SuperHi Plus, we are knocking down the walls of the SuperHi platform. You will keep ownership and agency over your content, brand, community, and earnings.

DIY
The do-it-yourself model

e.g. Patreon, Gumroad, Teachable or create your own

Risking your time and money

No support system to help and guide you

No in-built audience

Platform
The join-a-platform model

e.g. Udemy, Substack, Skillshare

No initial funding or an advance to pay back

Locked into their platform

Harder to build your own brand

SuperHi Plus
SuperHi Plus

Funding, support + tools
Funding, support, and tools

Clear, up-front funding with no need to pay back to support you

Direct support from experts who’ve made successful content

Own and control your content, branding + distribution
Own and control your content, branding, and distribution

Explore SuperHi Plus

Funding

Support

Tools

Your ownership + our funding

We aim to build a space where creators make sustainable incomes with their content. To kickstart and allow creators to focus on making the best content possible, we’re offering between US$6,000 to $12,000 without risk or repayment in return for 10–20% ownership.

You can make revenue through two main channels — direct sales and as part of shared access cards, such as SuperHi membership, where your revenue is based on the level of engagement your content receives across the creators.

View calculator

80%

Ownership split

Your ownership split is shared between all the owners of the content based on the percentage that they own.

Content ownership will be recorded on a blockchain to allow for an independently auditable way to view who owns what, along with tamper-proof records of ownership changes.

15%

Platform split

This is a 15% split of incoming revenue, which will go to anything that is integral to running the SuperHi platform. Such as paying staff, payment processing and hosting fees, as well as helping the ecosystem get continually better.

5%

Finder’s fee

To incentivize the whole community, we are giving a 5% split towards a finder’s fee – think of this a little like a reward that goes towards whoever helped promote or support the content you produce.

A more aligned revenue stream

With this co-ownership model, we aim to align incentives for creators, members, and the SuperHi platform.

For creators, SuperHi Plus de-risks making content by offering funding, support, and tools while they retain actual ownership and control.
Members will receive more diverse, high-quality content, knowing that creators are paid fairly.
For SuperHi, we want to help and support the most talented and emerging creators on the internet.

It’s in everyone’s interest to make high-quality creative content that people love. When you do well, we do well.

Your support system

Creating content can be an inten

description.pageDescription:
The page employs a bold, modern grid layout with a striking blue dominant color scheme. The layout is structured into distinct vertical sections that appear to slide or stack horizontally. The design uses a combination of white text on vibrant blue backgrounds, creating high contrast and readability.

Key sections include:
- Header with "SuperHi Plus" branding
- Three main content blocks labeled "Funding," "Support," and "Tools"
- Each section contains descriptive text and appears to be interactive/clickable
- Clean typography using Haas Grot Disp Web as the primary typeface
- Strategic use of white space between sections
- Circular decorative elements and geometric shapes
- Simple iconography including arrows and navigation elements

The interface features subtle hover states and appears to use horizontal sliding transitions between sections. Information is presented in a clear hierarchy with large headings and supporting body text. The design incorporates floating circular elements and abstract shapes that add visual interest without cluttering the layout.
description.style: Modern minimalist with a bold, contemporary edge. The design embraces simplicity while maintaining visual interest through strong color choices and geometric elements. The style feels fresh and digital-first.
description.audience: Creative professionals, digital content creators, and online entrepreneurs looking for tools and support to grow their creative businesses.
description.pageType: Product landing page / platform introduction page
description.layoutStyle: Modern and experimental, breaking from traditional vertical scrolling layouts in favor of horizontal section navigation. The approach feels innovative while maintaining usability.
description.intent:
The page aims to introduce SuperHi Plus as a comprehensive platform for creators, focusing on three key value propositions: funding, support, and tools. The design encourages users to explore these different aspects of the platform, with clear pathways to learn more about each offering. The ultimate goal appears to be converting creative professionals into platform users by highlighting the comprehensive suite of services available.

The minimal yet bold design choices communicate professionalism and innovation, while the clear sectioning of content makes the value proposition immediately apparent to visitors.
